Stacks are not multiplicative with each other, i.e. 2 stacks gives 10% more damage, not 10.25%.

This does not require the companion's ability to be off cooldown and does not put it on cooldown or invoke its mana cost.

Increased Area for Area Skills stats still only affect the area of effect of each individual meteor, not the size of the area in which they drop.

When you directly cast Glacier you are teleported to the target location, as long as the location is accessible. This does not count as casting the Teleport skill and is not affected by Teleport's skill tree.

Glacier also gains a cooldown and now counts as a {Traversal} skill.
Using a Traversal skill puts all other traversal skills on cooldown and traversal skills' cooldowns do not recover while you are using a traversal skill.

Both of these effects are multiplicative with other modifiers.

Low health is defined as below 35% of maximum health.
Both of these effects are multiplicative with other modifiers
Your bear has increased movement speed and deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of bleed on the target (up to 30).
Your bear's abilities have increased area of effect.
Your bear gains an additional charge for Earthquake. When you directly cast {Warcry} or enter {Werebear Form}, your bear restores a charge for Earthquake.

The base physical damage of your bear's melee attack is converted to lightning, and it has a {Lightning} tag instead of {Physical}.

Your bear shocks enemies on hit.

Your bear gains the ability to teleport towards enemies, on an 8 second cooldown.
If you have the {Pillars of Heorot} node, 100% of Poison Burst's hit damage is converted to cold.
{Torrent of Thorns} can still double the number of thorns in each burst to 2.
The stacks are not multiplicative with each other.

Frosbite inflicted by Serpent Strike lasts longer and has additional cold penetration

Each active stack of {Maelstrom} grants Serpent Strike additional chance to inflict bleed on hit and more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Armour Shred inflicted by Serpent Strike has an increased effect based on your Dexterity.
Serpent Venom deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) based on the number of poison and frostbite stacks on them, up to a maximum benefit from each ailment.

Serpent Venom deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) based on your melee critical strike chance.

If your melee crit chance is above 50% then Serpent Venom deals its damage twice as quickly. This does not increase the total damage it deals over its full duration, but rather halves its duration and doubles its damage per second.
Ailments applied by Serpent Strike and {Primal Serpents} have additional poison penetration.

Modifiers to projectile speed only affect the tangential velocity of spiralling hammers, not their radial velocity (i.e. they change the speed the hammers go around you, but not the speed at which they travel outwards).
The area of the rifts created by Abyssal Orbs scale with void rift area modifiers from this tree and with Increased Area for Area Skills stats.
Increased Area Stats no longer affect Area
Creativity stems from the simplicity of fire.
Fireball now pierces enemies (continue through targets it hits), but costs more mana.
Fireball hits have additional fire penetration and lightning penetration.
Improves Fireball's base critical strike chance.

Half of Fireball's base fire damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this portion of its damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Fireball gains a {Lightning} tag.

Fireball also gains additional critical strike multiplier.
Fireball deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and travels faster, causing it to travel farther.
Improves Fireball's base critical strike chance, but reduces its cast speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Fireball releases additional projectiles that fire in a cone when cast, but costs more mana.
Fireball releases additional projectiles. These additional projectiles are NOT reduced by the extra projectiles penalty via the {Embers} node.
Not affected by Embers' penalty to the number of extra projectiles
Fireball hits have a higher chance to ignite enemies.

Fireball hits have a chance to apply {Spreading Flames}, an ailment which deals fire damage over time and spreads to nearby enemies.

Additionally all damage over time ailments applied by Fireball deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifier).
{Spreading Flames} applied by fireball has additional fire penetration
Fireball hits have more critical strike chance against ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
More Crit Chance Against Ignited Enemies
Fireball hits have a chance to reduce enemy fire resistance.

Projectiles are fired all at once in a cone
Nodes on this tree that add crit chance instead increase stun chance by 20 times the stated value.

Fireballs fire in sequence in the same direction and can now hit the same target, but Fireball releases fewer projectiles and has less cast speed for each extra projectile (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Your Wolves have additional dodge chance, and have increased dodge rating for each point of strength you have.
Wolves have a chance to cause enemies to bleed with their melee attacks.
Wolves deal more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) and bleed they inflict has additional Physical Penetration.
Wolves have increased attack and move speed.
Wolves deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Your Wolves' melee hits deal more damage, have a greater chance to stun (both multiplicative with other modifiers) and it has improved base critical strike enemies, but has less attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Wolves have additional armor, and their attacks leech a portion of the physical damage they deal as health.
Wolves have a chance to chill enemies on hit.
Wolves deal additional cold damage with attacks, and have an increased chance to stun enemies.
Wolves have additional base health regeneration and increased health regeneration.
Your Wolves have a chance to retaliate with {Storm Bolt} when hit or when they hit an enemy.
Your Wolves have more health (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your Wolves deal more lightning damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your Wolves' melee attacks deal additional lightning damage.
Your Wolves now deal increase damage and have increased health for each point of Attunement you have.
This scales with your attunement, not the attunement of your wolves.
Wolves have more damage and dodge rating (multiplicative with other modifiers). This bonus is tripled for Wolves that have used {Howl} recently.
Recently refers to the past 4 seconds

When you kill an enemy one of your Wolves has a chance to {Howl}. This does not expend Howl's mana cost or put it on cooldown.

Howl also grants more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and increased melee attack speed to all affected allies.
The buffs granted by {Howl} last longer and wolves have a chance to Howl when they hit a rare or boss enemy. This does not expend Howl's mana cost or put it on cooldown.
Your Wolves' {Howl} also grant {Haste} for a short duration.
Bleed inflicted by Wolves lasts longer and wolves have a chance to slow on hit and when hit.

Your Wolves gain the {Ice Bite} ability, which deals higher damage in a larger area, with a 6 second cooldown. 20% of the remaining cooldown is recovered every time the Wolf hits with an ability other than Ice Bite, including their basic attack.

Ice Bite deals melee cold damage. Consequently this damage scales with your wolves' increases to melee and cold damage, but not increases to their physical damage.
Ice Bite has 100% increased radius relative to the wolves' basic attack.
Your Wolves gain a {Leap Attack} to close the gap on distant enemies. Leap Attack has a 10 second cooldown.
Your Wolves have a chance to retaliate with {Storm Bolt} when hit, and deal more spell damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
You have an additional wolf companion at all times. This wolf does not count towards your companion limit.
You can summon Wolves up to your maximum number of companions.
When you cast Summon Wolf, you will summon wolves up to your companion limit.
Wolves have a chance to {Howl} on kill. This does not expend Howl's mana cost or put it on cooldown.

When you attack with Swipe and hit at least one enemy you gain health if you are transformed, or mana if you are not transformed.

Swipe hits instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health). 

If you have a similar effect from an item or passive node then the highest threshold is used.

Each stack of {Aspect of the Panther} lasts longer and grants increased health regen.

Aspect of the Panther also applies to your {Sabertooth} if you have one.

Storm Claw Cooldown Reset on Totem Summon

You have a chance each second to cast {Lightning Blast} at a nearby enemy.

Disintegrate upgrades to the next Tier faster. This also affects the rate at which you gain stacks of {Lucomancer}.

Disintegrate deals more damage while at Tier 2, but also causes you to take more damage while at tier 2 (both multiplicative with other modifiers and each other), and these effects are are doubled at Tier 3.

You have a chance each second to cast {Fire Aura} while channeling Disintegrate.

For every 2 seconds you spend channeling Disintegrate, you gain a stack of {Lucomancer}, up to 2 stacks and each stack lasts 4 seconds. Stacks are refreshed when you gain a new one.

Lucomancer grants global more damage to ignited or shocked enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers) and global reduced mana cost.

You take less damage while channeling Disintegrate (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your skills recover their cooldowns faster while you are channeling Disintegrate.

Disintegrate now has two beams, and each beam deals damage independently, so enemies caught in both take double damage.

Disintegrate costs more mana per second to channel, and each beam deals less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Disintegrate deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but has a delay before casting. This delay is reduced by increased cast speed.

This affects all of your casts of Lightning Blast, not just from {Overload}.
Lightning Blasts from {Overload} have additional critical strike chance and a chance to chain additional times.
Discharge shocks enemies on hit. Each enemy is inflicted with 1 stack of shock for each 50 charges expended, up to a maximum.
After discharging, your next spell and melee attack deal additional lightning damage.
Additional Spell And Melee Lightning Damage
Discharge now has a chance to stun enemies, with a higher chance based the number of charges you have.
Nearby enemies are knocked back when you discharge.
Your next melee attack grants charges on hit. This effect has a 1 second cooldown.
You gain charges while moving much faster, but charges decay at your movement charge rate if you stop moving for more than 1 second.
Discharge has a significantly higher range, but can only hit enemies within a 90 degree arc.
You gain charges for each enemy you critically strike if you have discharged in the last 3 seconds.
Static's cooldown resets when you reach your maximum number of charges.
While over 80 charges, you expend 10 charges to cast {Lightning Blast} at an enemy within 8 meters every second. This effect consumes Lightning Blast's mana cost.
{Lightning Blasts} from {Overload} consume less of Lightning Blast's mana cost, but expends additional charges.
Maelstrom deals damage in a larger area.
Maelstrom deals more damage when cast on a totem (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Maelstrom has a chance to chill enemies each second.
Maelstrom and {Tsunami} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Maelstrom stacks have a shorter duration.
Maelstrom also heals allies in its area of effect each second.
Maelstrom deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but in a smaller area.
Maelstrom has an increased chance to freeze enemies each second.
Each stack of Maelstrom grants you increased dodge chance.
Increased Dodge Rating Per Maelstrom Stack
Maelstrom stacks have a longer duration.
{Power of The Storm's} stack requirement is reduced.
{Storm Bolt} from the {Power of the Storm} node is cast at additional targets.
Maelstrom deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). This bonus is doubled if you have at least 60 Attunement.

You have a chance on killing an enemy or hitting a boss or rare enemy to cast Maelstrom. 

This effect has a 2 second cooldown.
Chance On Kill To Cast Maelstrom
If you have 6 stacks of Maelstrom or more, you gain {Haste.}
If you have 6 stacks of Maelstrom or more, you gain {Frenzy.}
Each stack of Maelstrom grants you an increased chance to stun enemies.
Maelstrom itself cannot stun, as it is a Damage Over Time Spell.
Global Increased Stun Chance Per Stack
You can choose to cast Maelstrom on your minions instead of yourself.

When your number of Maelstrom stacks reaches 0, you have a chance to summon 2 to 4 {Twisters,} a spell that deals physical damage each second to enemies they pass through.

The number of Twisters is random, and the chance to summon them is multiplied by the highest number of stacks you reached.
Chance To Summon Twisters Upon Reaching 0 Stacks
When your number of Maelstrom stacks reaches 0, you release a {Tsunami,} a cold spell that deals damage multiplied by your highest stack count.

Maelstrom deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), has significantly increased healing effectiveness and lasts significantly longer, but you can have only one maximum stack of Maelstrom.

If you go one second without directly casting Maelstrom you gain a stack of {Lagon's Slumber}. When you next cast Maelstrom directly, all stacks of {Lagon's Slumber} are consumed to cast Maelstrom an additional time per stack.
Extra Casts per Stack of Lagon's Slumber

Maelstrom's base cold damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to cold damage.

Chill chance from this tree is converted to slow chance.

Maelstrom can no longer freeze, but each stack of Maelstrom grants global more physical damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Global More Physical Damage Per Maelstrom Stack
While you have 6 or more stacks, you cast {Storm Bolt} at a nearby enemy each second.
Storm Bolt scales with increased spell damage and lightning damage, but not with other nodes on this tree.
Storm Bolt Cast Each Second At 6 Or More Stacks
Maelstrom deals more damage to chilled or frozen enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
If an enemy is chilled and frozen, both modifiers apply multiplicatively.
Manifested Armor gains increased effect of stats from your body armor.
Manifested Armor has more health (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Manifested Armor gains additional health regeneration.
Manifested Armor reflects a percentage of damage to attackers.
Manifested Armor gains increased effect of stats from your helmet.
Manifested Armor deals additional melee and spell fire damage as well as more fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Forgebreath} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
{Forgebreath} is a spell that deals 60 fire damage to all enemies hit.
{Forgebreath} has additional ignite chance and a shorter cooldown.
Manifested Armor's melee attacks have a chance to ignite enemies.
Manifested Armor gains increased effect of stats from your boots.
Manifested Armor has a chance to taunt nearby enemies each second.
Manifested Armor has a chance to slow attacking enemies when hit.
Manifested Armor gains increased effect of stats from your gloves.
Manifested Armor's melee physical attacks have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
Manifested Armor deals more physical damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Manifested Armor's melee attacks have a chance to reduce enemy armor.
Manifested Armor deals more damage, but has less armor (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your Manifest Armor gains melee physical damage equal to a proportion of the armour on your body armour.
Melee Physical Damage per 10 Armor On Chest
Manifested Armor gains a shield which scales with the stats of your equipped shield.
Manifested Armor gains increased effect for health regeneration from all items that affect Manifest Armor.
Manifested Armor reflects damage back to attackers for an amount equal to your attunement.
Damage Reflected Per Point Of Attunement
Manifested Armor now also attacks with {Forgebreath}, a fire spell that deals 60 fire damage to all enemies in a cone in front of it. 3 second cooldown.

Manifested Armor gains the ability to use {Whirlwind Strike}, a spinning attack which hits all nearby enemies multiple times and travels a short distance. 

This effect has a 4 second cooldown.
Manifested Armor gains a charge ability, which deals physical damage and knocks back any enemy it passes through. 6 second cooldown.
Manifested Armor gains a sword which scales with your equipped weapon's stats.
Your raptor has additional elemental resistance and have additional dodge chance.
{Screech}'s cooldown is reduced, and it causes enemies it hits to {Bleed}.
Your raptor automatically reduces the cooldown of your other companions' activatable abilities by 3 seconds. This effect has an 8 second cooldown.
Raptor Reduces Other Minion Cooldowns Every 8 Seconds
Your raptor deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has increased area of effect for its abilities.
{Fire Breath}'s cooldown is reduced, and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Using a town portal will reset this buff even if you return to the same zone.
Your raptor deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
When a bleeding enemy dies within 12 meters, your raptor has a chance per stack of bleed on that enemy to gain Haste for 3 seconds.
Chance per Bleed for Haste on Enemy Death
Your raptor deals more melee damage per bleed on the enemy (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to a maximum.
Max More Melee Damage from Bleed
Your raptor's melee attacks have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
Your raptor deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your raptor's attacks have a chance to inflict blind, poison, and slow on hit.
Blinded enemies have 50% less critical hit chance and are less accurate at range. Does not stack.
Your raptor deals more damage for each percent of its missing health (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to 50% missing health.
At 2 points, this node gives the Raptor 198% more damage while it is at 1% health.
Your raptor gains increased effect from {Rampage}.
Does not increase the size gain from Rampage.
Your raptor has additional critical strike chance.
While your raptor is {Rampaging}, its hits instantly kill enemies below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).
Your raptor gains the ability to {Screech} at enemies, dealing damage and shredding their physical resistance. Screech has an 8 second cooldown.

Your raptor becomes a {Dragon Raptor}. The base physical damage of your raptor's melee attacks are converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not with increases to physical damage.

Your raptor gains the {Fire Breath} ability, dealing spell fire damage over time with an 8 second cooldown.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance for your raptor, and all effects in the tree related to bleed now depend on ignite.
Your raptor has increased attack and cast speed. This effect is doubled if it has killed an enemy in the past 4 seconds.
When your raptor hits an enemy, bleeds on that enemy have a 25% chance to double in speed.
Chance On Hit To Double Bleed Speed
Your raptor has increased melee attack speed, but loses 5% of its current health with each attack while above 50% of maximum health.
Percent Of Current Health Lost On Melee Attack Above 50%
Your raptor uses {Rampage} if it encounters a boss or rare enemy, if it is able to. This sets Rampage to 50% of its normal cooldown.
Raptor Rampages Near Rares And Bosses
{Rampage} also grants your raptor less damage taken for the duration.
Symbols increase the damage of you and your allies.
Symbols of Hope grants you and your allies increased mana regeneration.
Symbols of Hope grants you and your allies additional chance to electrify on hit.
Symbols grant you and your allies a chance to ignite enemies.
You and your allies gain increased critical strike chance per Symbol.
Symbols grant you and your allies increased healing effectiveness.
Symbols of Hope costs less mana and the active effect lasts longer.
You can summon an additional Symbol.
You have a chance to regain a Symbol after activation.
Symbol of Hope's active effect also grants increased healing effectiveness for its duration.
Symbol of Hope's active effect also grants Haste for its duration.
Activating Symbols of Hope now heals you for a portion of your maximum health.
Symbols grant you and your allies additional endurance threshold
You gain Symbols of Hope faster.
Directly cast Divine Flares which hit a rare enemy or boss have a chance per symbol consumed to recast Divine Flare.
{Divine Flare} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area per Symbol consumed.
Divine Flare now has a chance per Symbol consumed to cleanse negative ailments on allies, and a chance per Symbol consumed to blind nearby enemies.
{Divine Flare} now has a chance to gain a Symbol on kill or when hitting a boss or rare enemy.
Symbols of Hope has a shorter cooldown.
You can now cast Symbols of Hope's active effect on your allies. Any effects from this tree will now be granted to the recipient.
Symbols Of Hope Cast On Allies
Symbols grant additional block chance and block effectiveness.
You can summon an additional Symbol.
Symbols grant you and your allies additional fire damage.
When you activate Symbols of Hope, gain ward equal to a portion of your maximum health per symbol consumed.
Portion Of Health To Ward Per Symbol

Symbol of Hope's active effect creates a {Divine Flare} that deals fire damage to enemies in an area around you and heals allies for 50 health per Symbol consumed, but it no longer grants damage reduction.

Symbol of Hope's cooldown is significantly reduced, and it is no longer instant cast.
Symbols now only affect you, but provide twice as much health regeneration.
Fire damage provided by Symbols is converted to void damage. Each Symbol now grants 15% increased void damage, but no longer increases health regeneration.
Increased Void Damage Per Active Symbol
Symbols Do Not Increase Health Regen
Flame Ward's retaliation hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flame Ward's retaliation hits in a larger area.
Flame Ward grants armor while active.
Flame Ward grants additional block chance, block effectiveness, and ward on block while active.
Flame Ward's retaliation hits have a higher chance to freeze enemies.
Flame Ward costs less mana. Flame Ward and Fire Aura last longer.
{Fire Aura} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). This also effects Fire Aura from sources other than Flame Ward.
{Fire Aura} is a spell that deals fire damage over time to surrounding enemies.
Flame Ward grants ward over time while active.
Flame Ward grants increased ward retention while active.

Flame Ward grants additional fire damage to attacks and spells while active.

If Flame Ward is converted to cold or lightning, then this added damage is also converted.
You take less damage from hits while Flame Ward is active (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flame Ward reduces elemental damage taken while active (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flame Ward grants increased mana regeneration while active.
Flame Ward grants additional ward, but has a shorter duration.
Flame Ward grants significantly more ward, but no longer retaliates.
Flame Ward grants additional ward equal to a percentage of your missing health.
Percent Of Missing Health Granted As Ward
Flame Ward grants more ward, but has a longer cooldown.

Flame Ward grants increased fire damage while active

If Flame Ward is converted to cold or lightning, then this damage increase is also converted.
You have {Haste} while Flame Ward is active.
{Haste} grants 30% increased movement speed. Does not stack.
Flame Ward's retaliation is centered on the enemy that hits you, but it hits in a smaller area.

Flame Ward is converted to Cold. This converts the base damage of the retaliation, granting it a freeze rate, and converts any fire damage modifiers from nodes on this tree.

This also globally converts {Fire Aura} to cold and grants it a Freeze Rate.
Fire Aura Freeze Rate Per Second

Flame Ward is converted to Lightning, unless it has been converted to cold. This converts the base damage of the retaliation, and any fire damage modifiers from nodes on this tree.

This also globally converts {Fire Aura} to lightning, unless it has been converted to cold.
While active, Flame Ward grants you {Fire Aura}, a spell that deals fire damage over time to surrounding enemies.

Flame Ward can be cast on allies.

This has no effect if you have taken the {Mental Aegis} node.
Flame Ward is cast when you are stunned, if it is off cooldown and you have positive mana.
Mana now absorbs damage while Flame Ward is active. Each point of mana shields 5 health and ward.
Flame Ward can store an additional charge, allowing you to use it twice before it goes on cooldown.
Enemies around you are knocked back when you use Flame Ward
Earthquake and its {Aftershocks} have a chance to slow enemies in their area of effect.
Earthquake and its Aftershocks have a larger area of effect.
Earthquake and its Aftershocks deal more hit damage to chilled or frozen enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Hit Damage Against Chilled Or Frozen Enemies
Earthquake can cast more maximum possible Aftershocks.
Aftershocks have a chance when they hit to cast another Aftershock. Maximum of one additional Aftershock.

Chance To Cast Boulder On Aftershock Hit
Slows caused by Earthquake and its Aftershocks last longer.
Earthquake and its Aftershocks have an increased chance to stun enemies, and Earthquake costs less mana.
Casting Earthquake grants you {Haste} and {Frenzy} for a duration.
Aftershocks deal more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) and last longer.
Aftershocks blind enemies within their area of effect.
Aftershocks deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and have an increased chance to stun enemies.
Earthquake can cast more minimum possible Aftershocks. Each enemy can only be targeted by one Aftershock.
Aftershocks have a larger area of effect.
Earthquake's initial slam deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Earthquake's initial slam deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but in a smaller area.
Modifiers to Aftershock damage on this tree apply to the initial slam, but Earthquake can no longer create Aftershocks.
Earthquake's initial slam reduces enemy armor.
Earthquake's initial slam deals more hit damage against rare and boss enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Initial Slam Hit Damage Against Rares And Bosses
Earthquake's initial slam deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has an increased chance to stun enemies.
Earthquake's initial slam deals additional lightning damage, and Earthquake deals more lightning damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Casting Earthquake expends Storm Stacks over 0.5 seconds.

{Storm Bolts} triggered this way deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Stuns caused by Earthquake and its Aftershocks last longer, but Earthquake has a longer cooldown.
Earthquake's initial slam can't be dodged.
Aftershocks appear around your totems instead if they are nearby.
Aftershocks are now damage over time, dealing their normal hit damage each second. Aftershocks last 3 seconds.
Using Earthquake directly it now deals damage in a cone which has increased range. Aftershocks also favour targets in the direction Earthquake is cast.

Earthquake's initial slam occurs three times. First with 30% reduced radius and damage, then with 5% reduced radius and damage, then with 30% increased radius and damage.

Earthquake now has a cooldown, and each initial hit consumes mana.

Earthquake's base damage is converted to fire damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Additionally, armor shred chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance.

Earthquake is converted into a spell, and no longer scales with melee damage. Earthquake now deals substantial additional base damage on the initial slam and its base damage is converted to Lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increased lightning damage, but not with increased physical damage.

Aftershocks also deal substantial additional spell damage, but their damage is not converted to lightning.
Earthquake's initial slam deals more damage and has increased stun chance and stun duration, but Earthquake has a 6 second cooldown.
Arcane Ascendance grants you increased spell damage while active.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, each hit you take increases your spell damage and cast speed, but drains your mana.
You are granted increased spell damage each second while Arcane Ascendance is active.
Expires at the end of Arcane Ascendance.
You are granted increased cast speed each second while Arcane Ascendance is active.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, spells and attacks have a chance to slow enemies over 5 meters away and you deal more damage to enemies per stack of slow on them.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, you are granted mana whenever you kill an enemy that is over 5 meters away.
Mana Gained On Killing Distant Enemy
You are granted {Haste} for a duration after leaving Arcane Ascendance. The duration is capped at 200% of the time you spent in Arcane Ascendance.
Arcane Ascendance has a shorter cooldown.
You gain ward when you activate Arcane Ascendance.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, your spells and attacks that can freeze enemies have a higher chance to do so.
You knock back all nearby enemies when you enter Arcane Ascendance.
Lightning Blast cast by {Superconductor} triggers more frequently.
Arcane Ascendance drains less mana per second.
All of your spells cost less mana while Arcane Ascendance is active, but you cannot regenerate mana while Arcane Ascendance is active.
You can cast more spells via {Acuity}, but Arcane Ascendance has a longer cooldown.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, whenever you kill a frozen enemy a nearby enemy is frozen for a duration.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, spells and attacks have a chance to chill enemies over 5 meters away.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, spells and attacks have a chance to shock enemies over 5 meters away.
{Mark Of Thunder} deals more damage when it is consumed (multiplicative with other modifiers).

While Arcane Ascendance is active, you automatically cast {Lightning Blast} when you hit an enemy. Lightning Blast uses its skill tree and you lose mana equal to Lightning Blast's cost. This effect has a 2 second cooldown.
Arcane Ascendance now has a 2 second cooldown and no longer drains your mana. You can cast 4 spells before Arcane Ascendance ends automatically. Lasts 12 seconds.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, you {Mark} enemies you hit that are over 5 meters away. When you exit Arcane Ascendance, you consume all marks, dealing lightning damage to affected enemies.
You regain mana while you enter Arcane Ascendance, but the mana is lost when you exit Arcane Ascendance.
Eterra's Blessing grants elemental and poison resistance.
Your next spell after casting Eterra's Blessing deals increased damage.
Increased Spell Damage On Next Cast
After casting Eterra's Blessing, your spells leech a portion of the damage they deal as health for 4 seconds.
When cast on a minion, Eterra's Blessing grants stun immunity for 4 seconds.
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on your {Scorpion} companion, you are both granted a chance to poison enemies with attacks and spells for 4 seconds.
When cast on a minion, Eterra's Blessing also grants increased movement, attack, and cast speed for 4 seconds.
When Eterra's Blessing heals one of your companions it has a chance to refresh its activated ability cooldown, but Eterra's Blessing costs more mana.
When Eterra's Blessing targets a downed companion, it heals them for a portion of their maximum downed duration.
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on one of your companions, you also heal yourself for a portion of its base heal amount.
Eterra's Blessing has a chance to heal all allies nearby its target for 50 health.

Eterra's Blessing knocks back enemies a given distance from its target.
Eterra's Blessing has increased healing effectiveness.
Eterra's Blessing cleanses all ailments on its target.
When {Efflorescence's} heal over time expires, heal again for half of Eterra's Blessing's value.
When you are out of mana Eterra's Blessing is free, but Eterra's Blessing can no longer provide additional buffs.
When you heal a target that is already at high health, you have a chance to summon a {Healing Totem} at its location which heals nearby allies.
Targets above 65% max health are considered to be at high health.
Chance To Summon Healing Totem On High Health Heal
Eterra's Blessing heals for more if you have transformed recently (last 4 seconds).
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on your {Wolf} or {Bear} companions, you are both granted increased physical damage for 4 seconds.
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on your {Sabertooth} or {Raptor} companions, you are both granted a chance to cause enemies to bleed with attacks and spells for 4 seconds.
When you directly cast Eterra's Blessing on a companion it taunts nearby enemies for 4 seconds. However Eterra's Blessing now has a long cooldown.
Eterra's Blessing automatically targets the lowest health ally in range, but Eterra's Blessing costs more mana.
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on your {Spriggan} companion, you are both granted increased spell damage for 4 seconds.
Eterra's Blessing heals half of its value immediately, and half of its value over 4 seconds.

Whenever you directly cast Thorn Shield, you also cast Eterra's Blessing on the same target.

Consumes Eterra's Blessing's mana cost as rage.
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on your Storm Crow companions, they are granted {Haste} and Frenzy for 5 seconds.
Smelter's Wrath charges up faster to reach its maximum area sooner.
This does not affect Smelter's Wrath's damage while charging.
Smelter's Wrath hits have a chance to ignite enemies for each second channelled.

Smelter's Wrath has additional critical strike chance for each second channeled.

Additionally, it has additional critical strike multiplier for each second channeled.
Added Critical Strike Chance Per Second
Smelter's Wrath deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each second channelled.
While charging Smelter's Wrath you have more armor and endurance threshold (multiplicative with other modifiers)
Smelter's Wrath deals more melee damage and fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). These bonuses are increased by charging for longer.
Smelter's Wrath hits deal more damage (multiplciative with other modifiers) for each second channelled, but Smelter's Wrath charges more slowly.
Smelter's Wrath hits deal more damage to ignited enemies per stack of ignite, up to a maximum of 150%.  This effect is multiplicative with other modifiers.
More Hit Damage Per Ignite Stack
{Furnace} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Furnace} has a chance to ignite enemies each second.
{Furnace} has a chance to slow enemies each second.
{Furnace} has a chance to reduce enemy armor each second.
Smelter's Wrath hits have an increased chance to stun enemies per second channelled.

Smelter's Wrath charges up slower to reach its maximum area later (multiplicative with other channel speed modifiers).

Additionally, Smeler's Wrath gains additional physical and fire penetration per second channeled.
{Axe Volley} hits deal more hit damage per point of Dexterity.
Axe Volley Hit Damage per Dexterity
Smelter's Wrath hits deal more damage to rare enemies and bosses (multiplicative with other modifiers).
More Hit Damage Against Rares And Bosses
Smelter's Wrath hits instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health). This kill threshold is doubled at maximum charge.
If you have a similar effect from an item or passive node, then the highest threshold is used.
Smelter's Wrath now releases in a nova around you instead of a cone, but costs additional mana.
Smelter's Wrath hits have a chance to shred enemies' armor for each second channelled.
Smelter's Wrath has doubled crit chance and deals more damage when fully charged (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
More Crit Chance At Full Charge
When Smelter's Wrath is released, a duplicate attack is released in the opposite direction.
This node has no effect if you have the Detonation node.
Smelter's Wrath spews {Furnace} while charging, a fire spell that deals damage over time in a cone to enemies.
When releasing a fully charged Smelter's Wrath you also release five throwing axes in a cone in front of you, a throwing attack dealing physical damage to enemies hit.
Smelter's Wrath converts your increased attack speed to increased critical strike chance.
Increased Attack Speed -> Increased Critical Chance

When fully charged, Smelter's Wrath leaves behind a {Burning Patch} for 6 seconds which ignites all enemies who stand in it twice per second.

Smelter's Wrath costs additional mana.
Drain Life deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Soul Blast} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
Drain Life can now only target minions. Drain Life leeches a higher percentage of damage as health.
Drain Life deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but the health leech from its base effect and nodes on this tree is removed.
Drain Life has a chance to summon a {Soul Wisp} when it kills an enemy, a minion that deals necrotic damage to enemies.
You are granted ward when Drain Life kills an enemy.
Drain Life has a chance to slow and fear its targets each second.

You gain a stack of {Contempt} when you have channelled on the same enemy for 2 seconds.

Contempt grants more armour (multiplicative with other modifiers) and adds to all resistances. All stacks of Contempt are lost when you stop channelling.
You can only gain 1 stack of Contempt from each enemy.
{Contempt} stacks last for a duration after you stop channelling Drain Life.
The stun inflicted by {Stagger} lasts longer and Drain Life deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) against bosses and rare enemies.
Drain Life costs less mana per second.
Drain Life applies stacks of {Damned} each second. {Damned} is an ailment that deals necrotic damage over time.
{Damned} inflicted by Drain Life lasts longer.
Drain Life deals 3% more damage per stack of {Damned} on enemies affected by {Damned}, up to a cap (multiplicative with other modifiers).
If {Wandering Spirits} is active when you start channeling Drain Life it does not expire until you stop channeling.
Individual spirits still expire as normal, but new ones keep being revealed.
Wandering Spirits does not expire while channeling
Drain Life leeches a higher percentage of damage as health and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to targets that are on low health.
Drain Life leeches a higher percentage of damage as health, but costs more mana per second.
{Dark Shackles} lasts longer, but Drain Life costs more mana.
When a target is killed by Drain Life, you chill enemies in an area around them for a duration.
When a target is killed by Drain Life you cast Soul Blast at their location, dealing spell necrotic damage to enemies in an area around them.
Drain Life can now target your minions. You gain 10 mana per second for each minion you are draining.

Drain Life's base necrotic damage is converted to poison damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to poison damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

Swaps Drain Life's {Necrotic} tag for a {Poison} tag.
Drain Life no longer costs mana. Instead, it drains a portion of your current health per second while channelling. Nodes that modify mana drain now modify this health drain instead.

Does not affect the up-front mana cost added by the {Dark Shackles} node.

Does not cause your mana to regenerate while channelling.
Drain Life can drain an additional target.
When Drain Life has channelled on the same target for 2 seconds, it stuns them for 2 seconds.
Drain Life is now cast instead of channelled. Lasts for 3 seconds. Adds an up-front mana cost, and this cost is affected by modifiers to mana drain, except for the {Blood Pact} node.
{Dark Shackles} can hit up to 10 additional targets, and slows enemies hit each second, but Drain Life costs significantly more mana.
Anomaly hits enemies in a larger area.
Anomaly is cast instantly, but Anomaly has a longer cooldown.
{Time Wave} deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area, and increases to Anomaly's area also affect Time Wave.
An additional {Time Wave} is released when you cast Anomaly.
{Future Strikes} applied by Anomaly have additional Void Penetration
When enemies return to the present, all damaging ailments currently applied to them apply faster.
This affects the duration of ailments, not damage.
When enemies return to the present, the duration of damaging ailments applied to them is reset.
Enemies hit by Anomaly are afflicted with {Time Rot} when they return.
{Time Rot} inflicted by Anomaly lasts longer and has additional Void Penetration
When enemies return to the present, they take increased void damage for a duration.
Anomaly now applies Ignite instead of Time Rot. Modifiers to Time Rot apply to Ignite instead.

While you have Berserk, you gain additional stacks of Berserk on melee hit up to a maximum, and melee hits refresh all stacks.
Warcry now fears enemies for 2 seconds instead of stunning them.

Fear causes enemies to flee from you and your allies. Enemies can only be affected by Fear once.

This has no effect if you have {Jormun's Wrath}.
You now place down a totem for 3 seconds that repeatedly uses Warcry instead of you, but Warcry costs more mana.

Warcry cannot taunt if used by a totem.

When used by a totem Warcry benefits from your minion and totem stats rather than your own stats.
Warcry now pulls enemies instead of knocking them back.
Modifiers to knockback distance now affect pull distance.
Warcry now also taunts affected enemies for a short duration.
This effect does not work if Warcry is used by a totem.
Summons a Volatile Zombie which shambles towards an enemy and explodes.
Zombies now leap towards their targets, up to a maximum distance.
Zombies deal more damage and have more movement speed (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
Zombies deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and the Zombies' health decays slower, but they also move slower.
You now summon a single large zombie with more health, that deals more damage in a larger area (all multiplicative with other modifiers), but Volatile Zombie costs more mana.
Both the area and damage modifiers also affect {Repulsive Vomit}.

When a Zombie dies you have a chance to cast {Marrow Shards} from the Zombie towards a nearby enemy.

Marrow Shards does not cost health when cast this way.
No Health Cost For Marrow Shards
Zombies will now {Vomit} on enemies before exploding, dealing physical and poison damage over time. Zombies lose 33% of their maximum health per second while vomiting.

The damage of the vomit can be converted by the {Flammable Vitae} and {Horrific Vessels} nodes.

The area of effect of the vomit is not affected by Increased Area for Minion Area Skills stats.
The explosion applies {Mark for Death} to enemies hit, but Volatile Zombie costs more mana.
Mark For Death On Explosion Hit
You summon additional Zombies per cast, but Volatile Zombie costs more mana and has additional cooldown duration.
The explosion has a chance to poison enemies.
The explosion deals damage in a larger area.
The explosion deals damage in a larger area and when a Zombie dies it creates a {Skeleton Vanguard}, but Volatile Zombie costs more mana.
When a Zombie dies it creates {Corpse Parasites} for 4 seconds, but Volatile Zombie costs more mana.
The explosion deals damage in a larger area, but Zombies no longer move.

When a Zombie dies you have a chance to cast {Sacrifice} on another nearby minion.

Sacrifice cast this way costs less mana.
You summon Zombies faster and Volatile Zombie costs less mana.

You are healed for a portion of the Zombies' maximum health when they explode. 

The amount healed is affected by your increased healing effectiveness.
As an example, if the Zombie has 1000 max health, you are healed for 10 health when it explodes.

The explosion's base physical damage and {Vomit}'s base physical and poison damage are converted to fire. Consequently their damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to physical or poison damage.

Poison chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance and effects related to poison now depend on ignite instead.
No effect if you have taken {Horrific Vessels}.

The explosion's base physical and fire damage and {Vomit}'s base physical damage are converted to necrotic. Consequently their damage scales with increases to necrotic damage, but not increases to physical or fire damage.

Zombies instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).

If you have a similar effect from an item or passive node then the highest threshold is used.
The explosion now fears enemies for a short duration.
Fear causes enemies to flee from you and your allies. Enemies can only be affected by Fear once.

When a Zombie dies you have a chance to cast {Infernal Shade} on an enemy near the zombie.

Infernal Shade cast this way costs less mana.
Infernal Shade is a spell that causes the target enemy and all nearby enemies take fire damage per second.
Zombies have increased movement speed per point of missing health, but their health decays faster.
The explosion's hit deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) against bosses and rare enemies.
Hit Damage to Bosses And Rare Enemies
The explosion has an increased chance to stun enemies.
{Vomit} has a chance to shred enemy armor each second.
{Vomit} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Vomit} has a chance to poison enemies each second.
When a minion dies you have a chance to cast Volatile Zombie at its location, expending its mana cost.

This cannot be triggered by Zombies or Corpse Parasites.
This cannot summon more than 1 Zombie.
Chance to Cast Volatile Zombie On Minion Death
{Necromantic Fervor} now grants ward rather than health. The amount of ward gained is not affected by increased healing effectiveness.
Health -> Ward From Zombie Max Health
Summons a Volatile Zombie which shambles towards an enemy and explodes.
Storm Crows have increased cast speed, movement speed and more health (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Storm Crows hits have a chance to shred enemy armor.
Storm Crows deal more damage per active crow (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Storm Crows hits have a chance to shred enemy armor per point of strength, and armor shred they apply has increased effectiveness per point of strength.
Your melee hits have a 20% chance to grant active Storm Crows more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for 10 seconds, stacking up to 3 times.
Storm Crows have a chance to shock enemies and have additional lightning resistance.
{Crowstorm} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has an increased chance to stun enemies.
{Crowstorm} deals damage in a larger area and has a chance to shock enemies it hits.

Storm Crows' base lightning damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to lightning damage. It also has a chance to freeze enemies.

Shock chance from all sources is converted to {Frostbite} chance and effects related to shock now depend on Frostbite instead.
Storm Crows have a higher chance to freeze enemies.
Storm Crows deal more spell damage per stack of shock on the target (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to a maximum.
Your increases to lightning damage also apply to your Storm Crows, but at reduced effectiveness.

Elemental damage increases and increases that don't have a specific damage type also apply to the crows' lightning damage.

This node has no effect on non-lightning damage, even if the crows' damage type is converted.
Storm Crows' basic lightning spell has a chance to chain to up to 2 additional targets.
Storm Crows deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and have increased critical strike chance.
Storm Crows' critical strikes deal more damage.
Your melee hits have a 20% chance to improve active Storm Crows' base critical strike chance for 10 seconds, stacking up to 3 times.
Storm Crows' basic lightning spell deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each second they spent moving since their previous attack, up to a maximum duration.
You can now only have 1 Storm Crow, it deals more damage, has more health (both multiplicative with other modifiers) and is larger.
Storm Crows' basic lightning spell deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but now consumes your mana.
This effect does not work if you are out of mana.
Storm Crow hits have a chance to grant you a stack of {Aspect of the Crow} for 8 seconds, granting you additional melee and spell lightning damage per stack.
Your non-crow companions gain {Aspect of the Crow} when you do, but it has 50% reduced duration on them.
Aspect of the Crow On Other Companions

Storm Crows fly to your companions below half health to heal them. This effect has a 10 second cooldown.

The amount of healing is increased by healing effectiveness.

{Goodberries} now also grants the companion ward. 

The amount of ward gained is increased by healing effectiveness.
Storm Crows are able to use {Goodberries} more frequently.
Storm Crows can now also fly to and heal you with {Goodberries}.
This also takes the effect of the {Silverberries} node.

Every 10 seconds Storm Crows cast {Wisdom of the Storm} on its nearest ally, granting it {Aspect of the Crow}. 

This effect has 50% reduced duration on minions.
Wisdom of the Storm On Allies
You are granted additional Intelligence per active Storm Crow.
{Wisdom of the Storm} no longer has a cooldown, but can now only target totems.
Wisdom of the Storm On Totems
Storm Crows have additional dodge chance and increased dodge chance.
{Crowstorm} deals more damage per contributing Crow (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a shorter cooldown.
The damage bonus from Thunderous Blast is additive with Crowstorm's baseline more damage per contributing Crow.

Void Cleave hits ignite enemies and half of its base damage is converted to fire, meaning that this half of the damage scales with increases to fire damage but not with increases to void damage.

Ignite inflicted by Void Cleave gains additional fire penetration based on your increased void damage.

Void Cleave gains the {fire} tag.

All of Void Cleave's base void damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to void damage.

Additionally your next non-channeled fire attack or spell after Void Cleave deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Void resistance shred chance from all sources is converted to fire resistance shred and effects related to void resistance shred now depend on fire resistance shred instead.

Void Cleave loses the {void} tag.
Void Res -> Fire Res Shred Chance
Damage On Next Fire Attack Or Spell
When you use Void Cleave and hit at least one enemy, your minions gain a stack of {Molten Infusion} for 4 seconds, granting them additional melee fire damage and a chance to ignite enemies.
Minions only gain a stack of {Molten Infusion} when you directly hit them with Void Cleave, but {Molten Infusion} has significantly increased effect.
Molten Infusion Only On Minion Hit
You can travel farther with Void Cleave and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
After channelling {Warpath} for at least 2 seconds your next Void Cleave within 1 second deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Void Cleave deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in an increased area.
Improves Void Cleave's base critical strike chance and it cannot be dodged.
Void Cleave deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to enemies on high health.
Targets above 65% max health are considered to be at high health.
Void Cleave hits apply {Critical Vulnerability} and Void Cleave has increased critical strike chance.
Void Cleave critical strikes deal more damage and you gain mana with Void Cleave critical strikes.
If Void Cleave hits at least one enemy your next {Erasing Strike} is always a critical strike.

Void Cleave deals additional melee void damage. 

This effect is tripled for echoed Void Cleaves.
Void Cleave deals significantly more melee void damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to enemies on low health.
Targets below 35% max health are considered to be at low health.
Melee Void Damage To Low Health
Void Cleave hits slow enemies, have increased stun chance and increased stun duration.
Void Cleave has a shorter cooldown.
Void Cleave hits shred enemy void resistance.

You cast {Abyssal Echoes} after using Void Cleave.

Abyssal Echoes cast this way uses its mana cost.
Void Cleave hits inflict enemies with {Time Rot}.
When you use Void Cleave you gain {Ravaging Aura} for a short duration scaling with the amount of enemies hit up to 10.
{Ravaging Aura} deals additional spell void damage and has an increased area of effect.
{Ravaging Aura} leeches a portion of the damage it deals as health.
If Void Cleave hits at least one enemy your next throwing attack inflicts enemies with {Time Rot}.
Time Rot Stacks For Next Throwing
Void Cleave deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of {Time Rot} on the target.
Void Cleave hits shred enemy armor and {Armor Shred} applied by Void Cleave has increased effect and duration.
If Void Cleave hits at least one enemy your next throwing attack deals additional void damage.
You can store an additional charge of Void Cleave, but Void Cleave costs more mana.
Void Cleave deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of bleed up to a maximum.
This effect is based on ignite rather than bleed if you have the [Riftflame] node.
Your increases to physical damage also apply to fire damage for Void Cleave.
Armament stacks last longer and grant you additional melee physical damage per stack.
You have additional armor and block effectiveness per stack of Armament.
When you drop below 0 mana, you consume all stacks of Armament to restore mana per stack.
When you have 0 stacks of Armament you have an additional sword when using Multistrike, as if you had 1 stack.

Each additional sword is replaced with {Smite}.

Smite cast this way costs its full mana cost per cast.

You have a chance to retaliate with Multistrike swords on block. 

This effect has a 1 second cooldown and doesn't include the initial strike.
Multistrike attacks repeated by your {Echoes} have additional swords.

This only affects the echoed Multistrike, not the original use.

Echoing is a Void Knight mastery bonus. It grants you a chance for your attacks to repeat after a second. This node's echo bonus has no effect unless become a Void Knight.
The initial strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Multistrike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 30 armor, up to a maximum.
Directly using {Forge Strike} consumes all stacks of Armament to summon additional {Forged Weapons} per stack.
Multistrike has a chance to consume a stack of Armament while at maximum stacks to summon a {Forged Weapon}.
You now strike twice with Multistrike, but the full attack takes significantly longer. Each additional sword is now replaced with an axe that strikes its target twice.
This effect still counts as using Multistrike once.
Improves Multistrike's base critical strike chance.
When you create more additional swords than there are nearby enemies, each remaining sword can consume mana to strike the nearest enemy, even if it was hit by the initial attack.
This effect only works while you are on positive mana.
Multistrike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) while you are at maximum stacks of Armament.
Multistrike hits have a chance to inflict bleed on enemies per stack of Armament.
Multistrike deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of bleed up to a maximum.

Each additional sword is replaced with a spear, which stabs in an elongated area rather than a slash. This has no effect if swords are already converted to axes.

Additionally, Multistrike's critical strikes deal more damage while wielding a spear.
Multistrike deals damage in a larger area and its created swords can strike enemies further away.

If a sword kills an enemy it will create an additional sword to strike another enemy.

This effect cannot be triggered from additional swords created this way.
You can have an additional maximum stack of Armament.
You can have an additional maximum stack of Armament, but Multistrike has less attack speed per stack (multipicative with other modifiers).
While at maximum stacks of Armament and positive mana, attacking with Multistrike will consume all stacks and 40 mana to use {Forge Strike}.
Multistrike has an additional sword while you are at maximum stacks of Armament and wielding a two-handed weapon.
Additional Sword At Max Stacks With 2h
{Void Cleave} consumes all stacks of Armament to gain more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and increased area per stack consumed.
Javelin hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed and have a chance to slow.
Javelin hits deal more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to enemies the farther away they are, up to a maximum of 15 meters.
Javelin gains physical and lightning penetration for each second you have been standing still up to a maximum duration.

Partial seconds are counted towards the maximum bonus. For examble standing still for half a second grants Javelin 6% physical and lightning penetration with one point allocated. This is counted when Javelin is thrown, not when you start throwing.
Javelin deals more lightning damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and its hits deal additional lightning damage.

Javelin's base physical damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Ignite and bleed chance from all sources is converted to {Electrify} for Javelin and effects related to bleed or ignite now depend on Electrify instead.

Physical resistance shred chance from all sources is converted to lightning resistance shred chance and effects related to Physical resistance shred now depend on lightning resistance shred instead.

Swaps Javelin's {Physical} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
Javelin has a chance to {Electrify} enemies on hit. Electrify applied by Javelin has additional Lightning Penetration.
When Javelin hits an enemy it now splits into additional piercing lightning spears which aim towards enemies close to the target, but Javelin itself can no longer pierce and it costs more mana.
If there are no other enemies nearby the target, only 2 lightning spears will be created.

Javelin hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This effect is doubled against enemies on high health.
Targets above 65% max health are considered to be at high health.
Javelin hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) up to a maximum, for each enemy pierced.
Improves Javelin's base critical strike chance and its critical strikes deal more damage.
Javelin hits have a chance to shred enemy armor.
Directly using Javelin causes your next melee attack to costs less mana.
Directly using Javelin and hitting an enemy causes your next {Judgement} or {Forge Strike} to deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Javelin deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has increased attack speed.
Javelin hits have a chance to ignite enemies.
You now throw Javelin into the air causing 4 javelins to rain down at target location dealing damage in an area, but it costs more mana.

Now only 1 Javelin rains down at the target location, it deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and persists for 4 seconds, but has a cooldown.

It has a {Battle Standard} attached granting you and other allies in its radius increased damage and added endurance threshold based on your attunement.

You are limited to 1 standard.

If Javelin would echo, the echo instead throws down a Javelin similar to the {Siege Barrage} node, rather than another Battle Standard.
The {Battle Standard} now heals allies around it each second. The amount of health gained is increased by healing effectiveness.
The {Battle Standard} now lasts longer, but is still limited to one standard.
If an enemy walks over the {Battle Standard} it captures it and carries the flag until it expires.
This just means the {Battle Standard} aura moves with the enemy, not that they gain the buffs.
The {Battle Standard} now casts {Smite} at targets nearby every 1.5 seconds.
The {Battle Standard} casts {Smite} more frequently and Smite cast this way has improved base critical strike chance.

Javelin is now a combo ability, the first part throws down the {Battle Standard} and the second part causes you to {Dash} to the flag.

Javelin's cooldown is now longer.
This effect works even if the {Battle Standard} has been picked up by an enemy.
Additional Javelins rain down, but Javelin costs more mana.
Javelin deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Javelin deals more damage, but has less attack speed (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

Javelin now leaves behind a {Holy Trail}, that deals fire and lightning damage to enemies each second, heals you and your allies each second and lasts for 6 seconds, but Javelin now has a cooldown. The amount of health gained is increased by healing effectiveness
This has no effect if you have taken the {Siege Barrage} node.
Javelin deals more fire and lightning damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and you have a chance to ignite and electrify enemies with melee attacks while Javelin is on cooldown.
{Holy Trail} gains additional spell damage based on your increased healing effectiveness.
Spell Damage per 5% Increased Healing Effectivenes
Sunders the ground in a line in front of you, dealing damage to enemies along the path.

You now create an {Upheaval Totem} that attacks with Upheaval from it rather than yourself. You are limited to 1 of these totems. Upheaval now has a cooldown and costs significantly more mana.

Upheaval Totems last 8 seconds.

When you directly use Upheaval and hit at least one enemy you gain a stack of {Earth Shattering} up to a maximum, causing your next {Fury Leap} or {Earthquake} to consume all stacks to gain additional physical penetration and increased area per stack consumed.
This has no effect if Upheaval is used by a totem.
Buff To Next Fury Leap Or Earthquake

Upheaval now splits up into 3 fault lines in front of you, but Upheaval costs additional mana. Enemies can only be hit by 1 Fault Line at a time.

Upheaval Totems now have 50% less attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Upheaval now travels in 5 fault lines in a star around you.

Upheaval's base physical damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Upheaval has a chance to freeze enemies on hit.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to {Frostbite} chance and slow chance from all sources is converted to chill chance.
When Upheaval hits an enemy it has a chance to release a {Shockwave}, a melee physical subskill. The Shockwave cannot hit enemies already hit by Upheaval and vice versa.
Enemies hit by a Shockwave can not create a Shockwave, even if hit by Upheaval.
Upheaval costs less mana and has a chance to shred enemy armor.
Improves Upheaval's base critical strike chance and it cannot be dodged.
Upheaval critical strikes deal more damage.

Creating an Upheaval Totem consumes all active {Thorn Totems} to grant it more damage per totem consumed (multiplicative with other modifiers). You can now create an additional maximum Upheaval totem and you have an additional charge.
Upheaval Totem Damage Per Totem Consumed
You can now create an additional maximum Upheaval Totem and you have an additional charge.
Upheaval deals additional melee cold damage, and also deals more melee damage to frozen enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Upheaval has additional freeze rate and has additional chance to inflict Frostbite on hit.
You cast {Shatter} on enemies you kill with Upheaval, a spell subskill dealing cold damage in an area.
Upheaval now creates additional fault lines and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but costs more mana.

Upheaval hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed and have a chance to slow them.

This effect is doubled while using a two handed weapon.
Upheaval deals damage in a wider area.
This does not cause Upheaval to travel further.

When you use Upheaval and hit at least one enemy you gain a stack of {Earth Armor} for 6 seconds up to a maximum amount of stacks, granting you additional armor and endurance threshold per stack of Attunement.
This has no effect if Upheaval is used by a totem.
Endurance Threshold Per Attunement Per Stack
While you have {Earth Armor}, if you fall below 6 stacks of {Maelstrom} you consume all stacks of Earth Armor to cast Maelstrom once per stack consumed.
Consume Earth Armor Falling Below 6 Maelstroms
Upheaval deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but no longer scales with increases to attack speed.

Upheaval penetrates enemy physical and cold resistance.

This effect is doubled while using a two handed weapon.
Upheaval has increased area while using a mace, additional bleed chance while using an axe and additional cold penetration while using a staff.
Upheaval deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a higher chance to stun enemies.
Upheaval deals more melee damage to enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers) based on how close they are to you, up to a maximum.

This is fully effective within 2 meters and has no effect after 6 meters. 
This is based on your distance to the enemy, even with {Upheaval Totems}.
Upheaval hits have a chance to apply {Frailty} to enemies and it penetrates enemy physical resistance, but travels a shorter distance.
When Upheaval hits your totems it has a chance to release a {Shockwave} from the totem.
Totems hit by a Shockwave can create a Shockwave when hit by Upheaval.
Upheaval hits grant your totems increased armor and more damage (each stack multiplicative with each other and other modifiers) for 4 seconds. This effect can stack up to 4 times.
Upheaval and {Shockwave} hits from the {Sundering Strikes} node now {Shatter} totems, releasing shards of rock and debris dealing spell physical damage and heals you per point of Attunement.
Upheaval Totems cannot be shattered this way.
Your Armblade Slash, Dive and {Locust} hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.

When you create a {Locust Swarm} with Swarm Strike you consume all stacks of bleed from enemies hit by it, causing the {Swarm} to deal increased damage in a larger area per stack of bleed consumed.

The increased area scales up to a maximum.
Locust Swarm Increased Damage Per Bleed
Yours and your {Locusts'} hits have a chance to poison enemies.
You can control additional {Locusts} and your Hives contain additional {Locusts}.
Hives release {Locusts} more frequently and if you have an active {Locust Swarm} it also releases Locusts every second.
A Locust Swarm does not lose damage or otherwise change as it releases locusts, but cannot release more Locusts than were originally gathered to create it.
Locusts per 3 seconds from Locust Swarm

After using Summon Hive, Human Form is now replaced with {Cleanse Hives}, which releases all {Locusts} from up to 2 Hives, replaces them with {Healing Totems} and transforms you to {Spriggan Form}.

This requires you to have Spriggan Form unlocked.
When a {Hive} releases a {Locust} you regain health and {Rage}.
Your Armblade Slash can now hit your own Hives which causes them to release {Locusts}.

The base damage of Swarm Strike, {Locust Swarm}, and Dive is converted to cold, and {Locusts'} base melee damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to physical damage. These attack all also gain a chance to freeze enemies.

Poison chance from all sources is converted to {Frostbite} chance while in Swarmblade Form.

Your {Locusts'} Poison chance from all sources is converted to {Frostbite} chance.
You and your {Locusts} have a higher chance of freezing enemies per point of Attunement.
Your Hives now cast {Thorns} as if they were {Thorn Totems}, rather than releasing {Locusts}. They can cast thorns a limited number of times equal to the number of Locusts they would have released.
If you have the {Release the Horde} node, hitting a Hive will release thorns.
You and your {Locusts} have a higher chance to dodge and additional armor while in Swarmblade Form.

You cast {Maelstrom} a number of times when you Dive. 

This consumes a portion of Maelstrom's mana cost as {Rage}.
All of your dodge rating is converted to armor while in Swarmblade Form.
Your Swarm Strike, {Locust Swarm} and your {Locusts'} melee attacks all deal damage in a larger area.
Your {Locust Swarm} leeches a portion of the damage it deals as health to you.
{Locust Swarm} has more duration and Swarm Strike hits deal more damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

After using Swarm Strike, Human Form is now replaced with {Angered Swarm}, which adds 4 seconds to the duration of your {Locust Swarm} and transforms you to {Werebear Form}.

This requires you to have Werebear Form unlocked.
A random active companion uses its active ability after you directly use Swarm Strike.
Random Companion Ability After Swarm Strike

Your {Locust Swarm} deals more damage per {Locust} consumed (multiplicative with other modifiers) and while the Locust Swarm is active you have additional chance to poison on hit per {Locust} consumed.
Poison Chance Per Locust While Swarm Active

You and your {Locusts} deal additional melee physical damage while in Swarmblade Form and your {Locust Swarm} deals additional melee physical damage per active {Locust} consumed when you use Swarm Strike.
Swarm Melee Physical Damage Per Locust
You and your {Locusts} have improved base critical strike chance while in Swarmblade Form.
You regain {Rage} when you or your {Locusts} critically hit.
Rage Gain When You Or Locusts Crit

Your {Serpent Strike} tree now applies to Armblade Slashes, and it does not require a spear.

This effect does not include the base additional poison chance or increased poison duration from Serpent Strike.
Serpent Strike Tree Affects Armblade Slashes
You regain health and {Rage} when you or your {Locusts} kill an enemy or when you hit a rare enemy or boss.
Health And Rage Gain On Kill
Health And Rage Gain On Boss or Rare Hit

You gain a stack of {Howling Cascade} when you use Armblade Slash and hit at least one enemy. Reaching 6 stacks consumes them to cast {Tornado}.

This consumes Tornado's mana cost.
Gain Howling Cascade Stacks On Hit With Slash
Reduce the maximum stacks of {Howling Cascade} required to cast {Tornado}, and tornadoes cast via Howling Cascade deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Howling Cascade Stacks To Proc Tornado
When you use Armblade Slashes you now summon a {Locust} instead of doing a melee attack.
You have increased attack and cast speed while in Swarmblade form.
You can only control half as many {Locusts}, but they have significantly more health, deal significantly more damage in a larger area (all multiplicative with other modifiers) and are larger.
The maximum number of locusts is rounded up.
Swarm Strike and Dive deal more damage and you regain {Rage} on hit with both skills.
Rage On Hit With Swarm Strike And Dive
Each point of strength increases armour by 4% and improves skills that rely on the raw strength of you and your companions, such as Swipe and Summon Wolf.
Each point of attunement grants 2 mana and improves skills that rely on the innate magic inside you and your surroundings, such as Gathering Storm and Summon Thorn Totem.
You have increased health and every 3 seconds your next hit restores your health.

This effect can only proc if you are not on full health.

Your "hits" do not include damage over time or minion skills

You can dual wield melee weapons by equipping an axe or sword in your off hand. While dual wielding, you have increased attack and cast speed, but take more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Attack And Cast Speed While Dual Wielding
Your minions deal increased damage, and using a potion also heals your minions for a percentage of the amount it heals you.
Percent Of Potion Healing Received By Minions
Minion Melee Damage Leeched as Health
The healing from {Hunter's Restoration} also applies to your minions, and when you trigger Hunter's Restoration, your minions gain increased damage for 3 seconds.
You deal increased physical, cold, and lightning damage. These bonuses are doubled while you have an active minion.

Runebolt hits have a chance to shred enemy armor.
