Physical Spell Damage per Strength from Nearby Ally

Directly casting Runebolt now creates a {Runestone} in front of you if you do not currently have one nearby instead of casting a Runebolt. If you have a nearby {Runestone}, directly using Runebolt now casts two bolts from the {Runestone} towards the target.  

After several seconds {Runestone} consumes mana to explode.
{Runestone} does not count as a minion.
Runestone On Cast Instead Of Runebolt
Every second while channeling a skill, your nearest {Runestone} has a chance to cast Runebolt towards your target.
Runebolt now explodes when it reaches its target location or when hitting enemies or obstacles, dealing damage of the corresponding element in an area.
The explosion from {Devastator} deals damage in a larger area per uncapped resistance of the corresponding element.
Directly casting Runebolt and hitting at least one enemy has a chance to grant you mana.
Casting Runebolt and hitting at least one enemy grants you a stack of {Runeweave} up to a maximum.
Mana Spent Gained As Ward per stack
Each stack of {Runeweave} also grants you more elemental damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of armor shred on the target up to a maximum.
Elemental Damage per stack of Armor Shred
Maximum Damage Boost per stack of Runeweave
Each stack of {Runeweave} also grants your {Runic Invocation} additional critical strike chance.
Runic Invocation Crit Chance per stack

Runebolt gains additional effects for each unique {Rune} you have. Multiple {Runes} of the same type does not stack the effect.

{Rah Rune}: Runebolt has a chance to ignite enemies.
{Gon Rune}: Runebolt has a chance to shock enemies.
{Heo Rune}: Runebolt has a chance to inflict {Frostbite} on enemies.
Runebolt's combo is now cast in a reversed order.
Reverses order of Rune Bolt Combo
Casting Runebolt now randomizes the runes of your {Runic Invocation}.
This randomizes the element, number and order of {Runic Invocation}.
Runic Invocation Runes Randomization on Cast
Runebolt is now cast in a sequence with the same order of your most recent {Runic Invocation}
Rune Bolt Sequence as Recent Runic Invocation

If you have 3 {Runes} from {Runic Invocation} when you create a {Runestone}, the {Runes} are consumed to instead create an {Imbued Runestone} that fires three bolts. An {Imbued Runestone's} explosion has 100% more area of effect and damage.

This effect has a 20 second cooldown. When you cast {Runic Invocation} the remaining cooldown is reduced by 20% per {Rune} in the {Invocation}.
The {Glyph} explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of slow on the target.
Glyph of Dominion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and costs less mana.

You can now place down up to two Glyphs of Dominion, but Glyph of Dominion costs more mana. When the first {Glyph} explodes the second one does too. The second explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) the further it is from the first one up to a maximum.
Second Glyph Damage Per 1 Meter Distance
Glyph of Dominion expands slower and lasts a longer (both multiplicative with other modifiers). This increases the time before it reaches its full size to detonate.
Glyph of Dominion expands faster and lasts a shorter duration (both multiplicative with other modifiers). This decreases the time before it reaches its full size to detonate.

Glyph of Dominion expands faster and lasts a shorter duration (both multiplicative with other modifiers) per amount of missing mana. This decreases the time before it reaches its full size to detonate.

This effect also takes missing mana into account. For example if you are at -100 mana and have 200 maximum mana, you will gain 100% more expansion speed, which will cause it to fully expand and detonate in half the normal time.
More Expansion Speed Per 3 Missing Mana
After Glyph of Dominion its full size it now has a short delay before it detonates.

Glyph of Dominion now has a cooldown, which is refreshed whenever you directly cast {Runic Invocation}. It also deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per rune from {Runic Invocation}, without consuming them.

Glyph of Dominion now consumes the {Runes} from {Runic Invocation} on cast to gain additional effects per {Rune} you have. 

{Rah Rune}: Glyph deals more damage in a larger area per {Rune} of this type.
{Gon Rune}: Glyph detonates additional times during the delay per {Rune} of this type.
{Heo Rune}: Glyph inflicts stacks of Frostbite per second per {Rune} of this type.
Frostbite Stacks Per Second Per Heo Rune
Glyph of Dominion has additional charges and all charges are refreshed whenever you directly cast {Runic Invocation}.
You cast {Elemental Nova} in the middle of the {Glyph} if you stand inside it when the delay from {Doom Scribe} starts and you gain stacks of {Fire Aura}.
Cast Elemental Nova When Delay Starts
Glyph of Dominion's damage over time part deals more damage for each second it is active (multiplicative with other modifiers).
While standing on your {Glyph} you and your allies deal more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of Armor Shred on the enemy, up to a maximum of 14 stacks.
Damage Over Time Per Armor Shred
Glyph of Dominion deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Disintegrate} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and grants you ward per second if you start channelling it while standing on your {Glyph}.
Disintegrate Grants Ward Gain Per Second
Glyph of Dominion deals damage in a larger area.
Glyph of Dominion is now cast at your current location and it grants you and your allies ward per second per your uncapped resistance while standing on your {Glyph}.
Ward Per Second Per 5% Uncapped Resistances
You and your allies gain additional resistances while standing on your {Glyph}.
After casting Glyph of Dominion you gain {Static Charges} at an accelerating rate while standing still, until your {Glyph} detonates.

Whenever you or your allies enters your Glyph of Dominion it cleanses ailments and grants the {Haste} buff for a short duration.

Leaving and re-entering the same {Glyph} does not cause this effect to trigger again.
You and your allies gain additional Shock chance while standing on your {Glyph}.
Normal and Magic enemies have a chance to be stunned every half second while on your {Glyph}.
Stun Chance On Glyph Every 0.5 Seconds
Your {Lightning Blast} now chains while you are standing on your {Glyph}, but consumes mana when cast this way while you are at positive mana.

If {Lightning Blast} already chains, then this effect adds additional chains.

If {Lightning Blast} is channelled, then instead of adding mana consumption it is added to the channel cost.
Lightning Blast Additional Chains on Glyph
You and your allies gain increased area to spells and attacks while standing on your {Glyph}.
Spells and Attacks Area on Glyph

{Glyph of Dominion's} base lightning damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

Shock chance from all sources is converted to ignite for Glyph of Dominion and effects related to shock now depend on ignite instead.

Swaps {Glyph of Dominion's} {Lightning} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Ignite chance is halved for minions and other allies.
Glyph of Dominion ignites enemies on the {Glyph} every half second.
The ignite frequency from {Burning Symbol} is increased by your chance to ignite.
Increased Ignite Frequency Per Ignite Chance
Frost Wall has a chance to inflict a stack of Frostbite to enemies inside the wall every 0.25 seconds.

One of Frost Wall's {Pillars} now casts {Lightning Blast} at nearby enemies when you first pass through the wall.

This effect consumes mana equal to {Lightning Blast's} mana cost.
This requires you to be at positive mana when cast.

Frost Wall's outer {Pillars} cast {Lightning Blast} at nearby enemies every other second.

This effect consumes mana equal to {Lightning Blast's} mana cost.
After Frost Wall has existed for a short duration, its outer {Pillars} cast {Lightning Blast} more often, up to a maximum, every time an enemy passes through the wall.

After you pass through your Frost Wall your next {Runic Invocation} or {Glyph of Dominion} cast within 5 seconds is empowered. 

An empowered spell cast this way, costs less mana (multiplicative with other modifiers) and it also deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each second the wall has existed, before you walk through it.
Frost Wall now cleanses negative ailments on you and your allies when they first pass through the wall.
Cleanses Negative Ailments On Pass Through
Frost Wall deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a higher chance to freeze enemies.

Frost Wall now grants you and your allies ward and mana when they first pass through the wall.

These effects are doubled if the wall is placed at least 2 meters away from you.

Frost Wall now grants the {Haste} and {Frenzy} buffs for a short duration, up to a maximum, when you and your allies when they first pass through the wall.

The maximum duration is equal to Frost Wall's age plus 1 second.
Frost Wall now creates three {Runic Detonations} shortly after you pass through it, dealing spell cold damage to nearby enemies.

This effect doesn't destroy the wall.

{Runic Detonations} can be converted to fire with the {Pyroglass} node and to lightning with the {Crackling Barrier} node.

An enemy can be hit by multiple detonations at once.
The {Runic Detonations} from {Runic Conclusion} intensify, destroying the wall in the process and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
The {Runic Detonations} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area if you are facing away from the wall when the detonations occur.

You now cast Flame Ward on you or your allies (excluding their minions) that pass through your Frost Wall, if it has existed for at least 0.2 seconds.

Frost Wall now has a cooldown, which it shares with Flame Ward. Using one will put the other on cooldown.
Flame Ward On Ally Pass Through
If you haven't cast Frost Wall in the past 15 seconds, it has no mana cost and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Frost Wall is larger and lasts longer.
You deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to chilled enemies while you have an active Frost Wall.

Frost Wall deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) per second the wall has existed.

This does not affect ailments or subskills.
Damage Over Time Per Second Of Existence

When you cast Frost Wall while having an existing wall active with more than half its duration remaining, the existing wall is moved to the new target location instead of being replaced and you gain mana equal to half the Frost WallΓÇÖs mana cost.
This allows it to hit the same enemies or allies an additional time.
Frost Wall deals additional spell damage equal to a portion of your freeze rate multiplier.
Spell Damage Per 20% Freeze Rate Multiplier

Frost Wall's initial freeze has increased duration, and the wall deals more damage over time to frozen enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This does not affect ailments or subskills.
You are granted ward whenever enemies pass through the wall for the first time.
Ward Gain On Enemy Pass Through

Whenever an enemy passes through the wall for the first time, and after it has existed for 0.2 seconds, you gain ward equal to a small portion of the enemy's maximum health, up to a maximum amount of ward per enemy.

{Frost Wall's} base cold damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage.

For Frost Wall, chill chance from all sources is converted to {Ignite}, and Frostbite chance from all sources is converted to {Fire Resistance Shred}.

Swaps {Frost Wall's} {Cold} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Frost Wall now inflicts Brand of Trespass to enemies inside the wall. This Brand does not stack, but deals a large amount of fire damage over time.

Brand of Trespass deals more damage equal to your ignite chance.
Brand of Trespass On Enemy Pass Through

Whenever an enemy passes through your Frost Wall, you have a chance to cast {Fireball} towards them.

This effect consumes mana equal to {Fireball's} mana cost.
Fireball Chance On Enemy Pass Through

{Frost Wall's} base cold damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to cold damage.

For Frost Wall, chill chance from all sources is converted to {Slow}, and Frostbite chance from all sources is converted to {Shock}.

Swaps Frost Wall's {Cold} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
This node has no effect if Frost Wall is converted to Fire.

Frost Wall now casts {Runebolt} in the original target direction the Wall was cast. The element of the bolts match the element of the Wall, regardless of {Runebolt's} tree.

This effect consumes 2 mana per projectile and does not cast if you are at negative mana.
Flame Rush costs less mana and has a shorter cooldown. These effects are doubled if you have enough uncapped fire resistance.
Double Effects With 150% Uncapped Fire Res
Flame Rush deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 40 current mana.

Flame Rush now casts {Glyph of Dominion} at the target location instead of channeling and Flame Rush ends when reaching the Glyph.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of {Glyph of Dominion's} mana cost.
Glyph of Dominion At Target Location

Flame Rush now casts {Volcanic Orb} that is attached to you and only detonates when you end Flame Rush, alongside any other active {Volcanic Orbs}.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of {Volcanic Orb's} mana cost.

You take less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) during Flame Rush.

This effect is doubled against damage over time effects.
All buff effects from the Flame Rush tree persist afterwards for a portion of the time equal to the duration you spent in Flame Rush.
This includes effects like ward per second and the less damage taken during Flame Rush effect from the base skill.
Buff Duration Equal To Time In Flame Rush
You gain ward per second during Flame Rush and you gain a burst of ward when Flame Rush ends.

{Flame Rush's} base fire damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to fire damage.

{Fire Resistance Shred} chance from all sources is converted to {Lightning Resistance Shred} for Flame Rush and effects related to Fire Shred now depend on Lightning Shred instead.

Swaps {Flame Rush's} {Fire} tag for a {Lightning} tag.

Flame Rush now also casts a {Static Orb} in the opposite direction.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of {Static Orb's} mana cost.

{Flame Rush's} base lightning damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

{Lightning Resistance Shred} chance from all sources is converted to {Cold Resistance Shred} for Flame Rush and effects related to Lightning Shred now depend on Cold Shred instead.

Swaps {Flame Rush's} {Lightning} tag for a {Cold} tag.

Flame Rush now inflicts {Brand of Subjugation} to enemies you pass through. The Brand does not stack but deals a large amount of cold damage over time and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per your chance to chill.
Brand of Subjugation On Pass Through
Brand of Subjugation Damage Per 1% Chill Chance
You are granted the {Frenzy} buff for a short duration when you end Flame Rush.

You cast {Runic Invocation} when you exit Flame Rush, if you have it on your action bar.

This effect consumes mana equal to a percentage of {Runic Invocation's} mana cost.

Casting {Fireball} and then using Flame Rush in the same direction within 2 seconds causes that Flame Rush to deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area and grants you mana on cast.
Flame Rush Buff In Fireball Direction
Whenever you hit an enemy with {Fireball} during Flame Rush it causes a {Runic Burst} around the target.
This can trigger at most once every 0.2 seconds.
Runic Burst On Fireball During Rush
Flame Rush now applies {Fire Resistance Shred} on enemies it passes through.
This effect does not apply to the explosion.
Fire Shred Stacks On Pass Through

Whenever you pass through an ignited enemy you gain ward and consume all your ignite stacks on that enemy. 

Consuming your ignite stacks this way grants you ward per your ignite stack on them, but they don't deal their remaining damage.
Ignited Enemy Ward Gain On Pass Through
Ward Gain Per Ignite On Pass Through

Whenever you pass through an ignited enemy with Flame Rush, instead of gaining ward per stack of ignite on the enemy and consuming your ignite stacks without dealing damage, you lose 12 ward per stack of ignite and the ignite stacks instantly deal their remaining damage on the enemy.

If you do not have enough ward for this effect, a percentage of your remaining health is consumed instead, up to a maximum. Ignite stacks consumed this way deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per point of intelligence.
This effect takes into account ignite stacks inflicted by Flame Rush itself.
% Of Current Health Consumed Per Ignite Without Ward
You travel faster and further with Flame Rush.
Flame Rush deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
Whenever you pass through a {Frost Wall} during Flame Rush it causes the explosion to deal damage in a much larger area and its chance to critically hit is doubled.
Every 0.4 seconds during Flame Rush you create a {Rune Ember}, up to a maximum. Every 0.25 seconds while not in Flame Rush you fire a {Rune Ember} at a nearby enemy if you have any.
Fire Rune Embers While Not In Flame Rush
{Rune Embers} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Casting Flame Rush creates additional {Rune Embers}.
You now have a chance to create a {Rune Ember} on kill during Flame Rush and you create {Rune Embers} faster during Flame Rush.
Rune Ember Frequency During Flame Rush
Runic Invocation deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per {Rune} consumed.

When you directly cast Runic Invocation you leap backwards a short distance, hovering in the air for a moment. In this state you take less damage and directly casting Runic Invocation deals more damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers). 

The effect of {Enigma} no longer works when taking this node.

Runic Invocation gains the {Movement} tag.

When you directly cast Runic Invocation now leap forwards instead of backwards. 

The effect of {Enigma} no longer works when taking this node.

Runic Invocation gains the {Movement} tag.

During the leap you also cast the Invocation corresponding to your first two {Runes}, consuming its mana cost.

Your Invocation must have at least two runes for this effect to occur.

During the leap you also cast the Invocation corresponding to your first {Rune}, consuming its mana cost.

Your Invocation must have at least one rune for this effect to occur.
Runic Invocation grants ward per {Rune} consumed and on direct casts you refund a portion of its mana cost.
Runic Invocation has increased cast speed and when directly casting an {Invocation} you gain ward equal to a portion of your current mana.
When you directly cast Runic Invocation, the next non-channelled elemental spell you cast penetrates enemy elemental resistance and deals damage in a larger area.
When you consume {Runes} with a skill other than Runic Invocation you have a chance to also cast the corresponding {Invocation} for those runes.
Invocations Chance On Other Skill Use
{Invocations} that deal damage in an area now deal damage in a larger area.
{Invocations} that include at least one {Rah Rune} have a chance to ignite enemies and deal additional spell fire damage.
Spell Fire Damage With Rah Rune

Runes now provide you with passive effects while you have them.

{Rah Rune}: Grants increased mana regeneration per {Rune} of this type.
{Gon Rune}: Grants ward per second per {Rune} of this type.
{Heo Rune}: Grants a higher chance to freeze enemies per {Rune} of this type.
Increased Mana Regen per Rah Rune
Ward Gain Per Second per Gon Rune
Freeze Rate Multiplier per Heo Rune

Every second you go without casting a Runic Invocation you gain a stack of {Runic Energy} up to a maximum. {Invocations} consume all {Runic Energy} to deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per {Runic Energy}.
Damage Per Stack Of Runic Energy

When you directly cast Runic Invocation with at least 12 {Runic Energy} you also proc a random equipped non-channeling skill of the same element as each of the {Runes} consumed.

This effect consumes these skills' mana costs and puts them on cooldown (excluding movement skills and skills that are currently on cooldown).

These skills are cast at 0.24 second intervals after the {Invocation}.
Cast Random Equipped Skill With 12 Energy
Runic Invocation now has a chance to be repeated per stack of {Runic Energy}.
While you have 3 runes and your combination of runes changes without casting an invocation, you gain {Runic Energy}.
Gain Runic Energy While At 3 Runes
Your maximum {Runic Energy} is tripled, but bonuses per {Runic Energy} are halved. When you cast a skill you gain {Runic Energy} per mana spent.
Each unique {Invocation} you directly cast causes {Runic Energy} to accumulate faster until you repeat an {Invocation}.
Accumulation Speed Resets On Repeat Invocation
{Invocations} that include at least one {Heo Rune} have a chance to chill enemies and have a higher chance to freeze them.
Freeze Rate Multiplier With Heo Rune
Directly casting Runic Invocation now refunds its mana cost and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but it now has a cooldown when cast this way.
You cannot refund more than 100% of Runic Invocation's mana cost.

Whenever you gain a {Rune}, instead of it depending on the type of skill cast, it always results in your sequence of {Runes} mirroring the first three skills on your action bar from left to right.

This effect ignores skills that don't have an elemental tag.

If a skill has multiple elements, the {Rune} it provides is chosen based on the least common elements among all 3 skills. 

For example casting {Disintegrate, Lightning Blast, Static Orb} in a sequence gives {Rah, Gon and Gon Runes}.

This effect ignores Runic Invocation itself when looking at the abilities on your bar.
Rune Gain Depend On Skills On Action Bar
Runic Invocation has a shorter cooldown.
While you have 3 {Runes} and your combination of {Runes} changes without casting an {Invocation}, Runic Invocation's remaining cooldown is reduced.
Cooldown Reduction When Rune Combination Changes

Whenever you directly cast an {Invocation} you blink to the target location first, but Runic Invocation now has a cooldown. 

{Invocations} cast this way automatically targets a random nearby enemy.

Runic Invocation gains the {Traversal} tag.
All the effects of this node do not affect Runic Invocation if you have taken the {Stride to Safety} node.

After casting Runic Invocation you gain additional spell damage and cast speed for a short duration. 

The buff duration scales with amount of {Runes} consumed.
{Invocations} that include at least one {Gon Rune} have a chance to shock enemies and have a higher chance to critically hit enemies.
Critical Strike Chance With Gon Rune
Runic Invocation deals more damage per type of {Rune} consumed.
When you cast an {Invocation} using a sequence of three different {Runes} you gain ward per point of intelligence.
Ward Gain Per Intelligence With Different Runes

Whenever you cast Runic Invocation, you also cast up to one of each type of {Elemental Nova} that you can cast directly. Each unique {Rune} you have when casting Runic Invocation represents different {Elemental Nova}. 

These {Novas} are cast with a 0.3 second interval.

This effect consumes mana equal to {Elemental Nova's} mana cost.

{Rah Rune}: Fire Nova
{Gon Rune}: Lightning Nova
{Heo Rune}: Ice Nova

If you don't have any of the three Elemental Nova types unlocked, then you'll cast one Elemental Nova if you have at least one rune.
Cast Elemental Novas From Runes In Invocation

You gain {Runes} faster while channelling.

You gain a burst of ward based on your maximum mana when your number of runes increases from channelling.
Each point of intelligence grants 4% ward retention and improves skills that rely on carefully studied magic, such as Lightning Blast and Mana Strike.
You have additional health and you take less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) from ignited, shocked or chilled enemies.
The damage reduction only applies once even if an enemy is afflicted by more than one of these ailments.
Less Damage Taken from Ignited Shocked or Chilled Enemies

Skills cast during the cooldown do not count towards this effect.

Ward is a shield that protects your health. It has no maximum and decays over time.

Ward is a shield that protects your health. It has no maximum and decays over time.

You gain ward when you critically strike enemies.

Frost Claw's deals additional spell damage based on your added melee damage.
The type of added melee damage determines the type of spell damage gained. For example if you have +30 Melee Void Damage, Frost Claw will gain +10 Spell Void Damage.
Frost Claw's explosion deals damage in a larger area. This bonus is doubled if you have at least 1200 ward.
Frost Claw costs less mana and you have a chance to regain mana when you cast it.
Chance to gain 12 mana on Cast

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Chill chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to lightning, unless it has already been converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Frostbite chance from all sources is converted to shock chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
Frost Claw hits have a chance to {Slow} and {Chill}.
You have increased cast speed with Frost Claw and when you cast Frost Claw you cleanse a random set of negative ailments on you.
All stacks of a chosen negative ailment are cleansed at once. Negative ailments include, but are not limited to, ignite, chill, slow, poison, and shock.
Frost Claw hits have a chance to inflicts {Frostbite}.
Frostbite, chill, slow, ignite, and shock inflicted by Frost Claw last longer

You have a chance to cast {Elemental Nova} when Frost Claw's projectiles reach the target.

This consumes mana equal to elemental nova's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.
The chance is once per cast, not once per projectile. This cast of eleemntal nova scales with your elemental nova tree, not this tree.
Frost Claw's has a higher chance to freeze based on its critical strike chance.
Freeze Rate Multiplier per 1% Crit Chance
If you have an active {Ice Barrage} when you hit an enemy with Frost Claw, that Ice Barrage fires faster for the rest of its duration, up to a maximum.
This fire rate modifier is multiplicative with other modifiers.
Ice Barrage Rate of Fire on Hit

You have a chance to cast {Ice Barrage} in the target direction when you cast Frost Claw.

This consumes mana equal to Ice Barrage's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.

When you are hit by an enemy you have a chance to retaliate with Frost Claw.

When you cast Frost Claw by any method you gain Ward.
When you cast Frost Claw you gain ward based on its Freeze Rate Multiplier.
Addtional Freeze Rate Multiplier from this tree, including from the Cold and Calculating, is taken into account for this node.
When you use a Melee Attack and hit at least one enemy you have a chance to cast Frost Claw at it, or at another nearby enemy if the hit killed the original target.
Gain a stack of Frozen Sleeper every second up to a maximum. When you cast Frost Claw it consumes all stacks to gain cold penetration per stack and release an {Ice Spiral} for each stack.
Ice Spirals on Cast per Stack
When you cast {Ice Spirals} there is a chance that the number of projectiles will be doubled and they'll all deal double damage.
The chance is calculated when Ice Spirals are cast and either applies to all of them or none of them with each cast.
For each active {Ice Spiral}, Glacier gains increased area and additional cold penetration, and Snap Freeze gains increased area and freeze duration.
Glacier Cold Penetration per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Area per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Freeze Duration per Ice Spiral

Directly casting Frost Claw causes it to recast from the target location to a location 4 metres behind it. The second can hit the same enemies as the first cast.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} causes a third cast. The third cast can hit the same enemies as the first and second casts.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.
The third cast is aimed at a location 4 metres behind the second cast.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} targets a random enemy that is 3 to 9 metres away from the original target location.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

When the second cast from {On Through The Snow} is released, Frost Claw is also cast back towards you.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.
The chance is calculated when Ice Spirals are cast and either applies to all of them or none of them with each cast.

Frost Claw has 2 additional projectiles allowing it to hit enemies in a wider area on the way to the target.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

Frost Claw no longer creates a burst of damage at the target, but its projectiles are now staggered and they can all hit the same target.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.
Frost Claw has a lower mana cost and it deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to frozen enemies, but its projectiles are slower.
Frost Claw's projectiles are faster and it costs less mana.

The additional Endurance Threshold applies to attacks from individual enemies based on how many stacks of Ignite are on that enemy. As an example, if an enemy has 10 stacks of Ignite, you have 20 additional Endurance Threshold against that enemy's attacks.
You will always reach your target after 1.5 seconds, but modifiers to travel speed affect the maximum possible distance.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

Frost Claw no longer creates a burst of damage at the target, but its projectiles are now staggered and they can all hit the same target.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.
Frost Claw has a lower mana cost and it deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to frozen enemies, but its projectiles are slower.
Frost Claw's projectiles are faster and it costs less mana.

The additional Endurance Threshold applies to attacks from individual enemies based on how many stacks of Ignite are on that enemy. As an example, if an enemy has 10 stacks of Ignite, you have 20 additional Endurance Threshold against that enemy's attacks.
You will always reach your target after 1.5 seconds, but modifiers to travel speed affect the maximum possible distance.
Runic Invocation penetrates elemental resistances per point of Intelligence.
Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze enemies and a higher chance to critically strike.
Frost Claw deals additional spell damage based on your added melee damage.
The type of added melee damage determines the type of spell damage gained. For example if you have +30 Melee Void Damage, Frost Claw will gain +10 Spell Void Damage.
Frost Claw's explosion deals damage in a larger area. This bonus is doubled if you have at least 1200 ward.
Frost Claw costs less mana and you have a chance to regain mana when you cast it.
Chance to gain 12 mana on Cast

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Chill chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to lightning, unless it has already been converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Frostbite chance from all sources is converted to shock chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
Frost Claw hits have a chance to {Slow} and {Chill}.
You have increased cast speed with Frost Claw and when you cast Frost Claw you cleanse a random set of negative ailments on you.
All stacks of a chosen negative ailment are cleansed at once. Negative ailments include, but are not limited to, ignite, chill, slow, poison, and shock.
Frost Claw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and its hits have a chance to inflict {Frostbite}.
Frostbite, chill, slow, ignite, and shock inflicted by Frost Claw last longer.

You have a chance to cast {Elemental Nova} when Frost Claw's projectiles reach the target.

This consumes mana equal to Elemental Nova's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.
The chance is once per cast, not once per projectile.
Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze based on its critical strike chance.
Freeze Rate Multiplier per 1% Crit Chance
If you have an active {Ice Barrage} when you hit an enemy with Frost Claw, that Ice Barrage fires faster for the rest of its duration, up to a maximum.
This fire rate modifier is multiplicative with other modifiers.
Ice Barrage Rate of Fire on Hit

You have a chance to cast {Ice Barrage} in the target direction when you cast Frost Claw.

This consumes mana equal to Ice Barrage's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.

When you are hit by an enemy you have a chance to retaliate with Frost Claw.

When you cast Frost Claw by any method you gain Ward.
When you cast Frost Claw you gain ward based on its Freeze Rate Multiplier.
Addtional Freeze Rate Multiplier from this tree, including from Cold and Calculating, is taken into account for this node.
When you use a Melee Attack and hit at least one enemy you have a chance to cast Frost Claw at it, or at another nearby enemy if the hit killed the original target.
Gain a stack of Frozen Sleeper every second up to a maximum. When you cast Frost Claw it consumes all stacks to gain cold penetration per stack and release an {Ice Spiral} for each stack.
Ice Spirals on Cast per Stack
When you cast {Ice Spirals} there is a chance that the number of projectiles will be doubled and they'll all deal double damage.
The chance is calculated when Ice Spirals are cast and either applies to all of them or none of them with each cast.
For each active {Ice Spiral}, Glacier gains increased area and additional cold penetration, and Snap Freeze gains increased area and freeze duration.
These bonuses apply based on the number of your Ice Spirals that exist when you begin casting Glacier or Snap Freeze.
Glacier Cold Penetration per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Area per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Freeze Duration per Ice Spiral

Directly casting Frost Claw causes it to recast from the target location to a location 4 meters behind it. The second can hit the same enemies as the first cast.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} causes a third cast. The third cast can hit the same enemies as the first and second casts.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.
The third cast is aimed at a location 4 meters behind the second cast.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} targets a random enemy that is 3 to 9 meters away from the original target location.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

When the second cast from {On Through The Snow} is released, Frost Claw is also cast back towards you.

When you cast {Ice Spirals} there is a chance that the number of projectiles will be doubled and they'll all deal double damage.
The chance is calculated when Ice Spirals are cast and either applies to all of them or none of them with each cast.
For each active {Ice Spiral}, Glacier gains increased area and additional cold penetration, and Snap Freeze gains increased area and freeze duration.
These bonuses apply based on the number of your Ice Spirals that exist when you begin casting Glacier or Snap Freeze.
Glacier Cold Penetration per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Area per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Freeze Duration per Ice Spiral

Directly casting Frost Claw causes it to recast from the target location to a location 4 meters behind it. The second can hit the same enemies as the first cast.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} causes a third cast. The third cast can hit the same enemies as the first and second casts.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.
The third cast is aimed at a location 4 meters behind the second cast.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} targets a random enemy that is 3 to 9 meters away from the original target location.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

When the second cast from {On Through The Snow} is released, Frost Claw is also cast back towards you.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.

Frost Claw has 2 additional projectiles allowing it to hit enemies in a wider area on the way to the target.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

Frost Claw no longer creates a burst of damage at the target, but its projectiles are now staggered and they can all hit the same target.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.
Frost Claw has a lower mana cost and it deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to frozen enemies, but its projectiles are slower.
Frost Claw's projectiles are faster and it costs less mana.

The additional Endurance Threshold applies to attacks from individual enemies based on how many stacks of Ignite are on that enemy. As an example, if an enemy has 10 stacks of Ignite, you have 20 additional Endurance Threshold against that enemy's attacks.
You will always reach your target after 1.5 seconds, but modifiers to travel speed affect the maximum possible distance.
Runic Invocation penetrates elemental resistances per point of Intelligence.
Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze enemies and a higher chance to critically strike.
Frost Claw deals additional spell damage based on your added melee damage.
The type of added melee damage determines the type of spell damage gained. For example if you have +30 Melee Void Damage, Frost Claw will gain +10 Spell Void Damage.
Frost Claw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and its explosion deals damage in a larger area. These bonuses are doubled if you have at least 1200 ward.
Frost Claw costs less mana and you have a chance to regain mana when you cast it.
Chance to gain 12 mana on Cast

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Chill chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to lightning, unless it has already been converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Frostbite chance from all sources is converted to shock chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
Frost Claw hits have a chance to {Slow} and {Chill}.
You have increased cast speed with Frost Claw and when you cast Frost Claw you cleanse a random set of negative ailments on you.
All stacks of a chosen negative ailment are cleansed at once. Negative ailments include, but are not limited to, ignite, chill, slow, poison, and shock.
Frost Claw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and its hits have a chance to inflict {Frostbite}.
Frostbite, chill, slow, ignite, and shock inflicted by Frost Claw last longer.

You have a chance to cast {Elemental Nova} when Frost Claw's projectiles reach the target.

This consumes mana equal to Elemental Nova's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.
The chance is once per cast, not once per projectile.
Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze based on its critical strike chance.
Freeze Rate Multiplier per 1% Crit Chance
If you have an active {Ice Barrage} when you hit an enemy with Frost Claw, that Ice Barrage fires faster for the rest of its duration, up to a maximum.
This fire rate modifier is multiplicative with other modifiers.
Ice Barrage Rate of Fire on Hit

You have a chance to cast {Ice Barrage} in the target direction when you cast Frost Claw.

This consumes mana equal to Ice Barrage's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.

When you are hit by an enemy you have a chance to retaliate with Frost Claw.

When you cast Frost Claw by any method you gain Ward.
When you cast Frost Claw you gain ward based on its Freeze Rate Multiplier.
Addtional Freeze Rate Multiplier from this tree, including from Cold and Calculating, is taken into account for this node.
When you use a Melee Attack and hit at least one enemy you have a chance to cast Frost Claw at it, or at another nearby enemy if the hit killed the original target.

These bonuses apply based on the number of your Ice Spirals that exist when you begin casting Glacier or Snap Freeze.

Frostbite, chill, slow, ignite, and shock inflicted by Frost Claw last longer.

You have a chance to cast {Elemental Nova} when Frost Claw's projectiles reach the target.

This consumes mana equal to Elemental Nova's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.
The chance is once per cast, not once per projectile.
Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze based on its critical strike chance.
Freeze Rate Multiplier per 1% Crit Chance
If you have an active {Ice Barrage} when you hit an enemy with Frost Claw, that Ice Barrage fires faster for the rest of its duration, up to a maximum.
This fire rate modifier is multiplicative with other modifiers.
Ice Barrage Rate of Fire on Hit

You have a chance to cast {Ice Barrage} in the target direction when you cast Frost Claw.

This consumes mana equal to Ice Barrage's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.

When you are hit by an enemy you have a chance to retaliate with Frost Claw.

When you cast Frost Claw by any method you gain Ward.
When you cast Frost Claw you gain ward based on its Freeze Rate Multiplier.
Addtional Freeze Rate Multiplier from this tree, including from Cold and Calculating, is taken into account for this node.
When you use a Melee Attack and hit at least one enemy you have a chance to cast Frost Claw at it, or at another nearby enemy if the hit killed the original target.
Gain a stack of Frozen Sleeper every second up to a maximum. When you cast Frost Claw it consumes all stacks to gain cold penetration per stack and release an {Ice Spiral} for each stack.
Ice Spirals on Cast per Stack
When you cast {Ice Spirals} there is a chance that the number of projectiles will be doubled and they'll all deal double damage.
The chance is calculated when Ice Spirals are cast and either applies to all of them or none of them with each cast.
For each active {Ice Spiral}, Glacier gains increased area and additional cold penetration, and Snap Freeze gains increased area and freeze duration.
These bonuses apply based on the number of your Ice Spirals that exist when you begin casting Glacier or Snap Freeze.
Glacier Cold Penetration per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Area per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Freeze Duration per Ice Spiral

Directly casting Frost Claw causes it to recast from the target location to a location 4 meters behind it. The second can hit the same enemies as the first cast.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} causes a third cast. The third cast can hit the same enemies as the first and second casts.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.
The third cast is aimed at a location 4 meters behind the second cast.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} targets a random enemy that is 3 to 9 meters away from the original target location.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

When the second cast from {On Through The Snow} is released, Frost Claw is also cast back towards you.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.

Frost Claw has 2 additional projectiles allowing it to hit enemies in a wider area on the way to the target.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

Frost Claw no longer creates a burst of damage at the target, but its projectiles are now staggered and they can all hit the same target.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.
Frost Claw has a lower mana cost and it deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to frozen enemies, but its projectiles are slower.
Frost Claw's projectiles are faster and it costs less mana.

The additional Endurance Threshold applies to attacks from individual enemies based on how many stacks of Ignite are on that enemy. As an example, if an enemy has 10 stacks of Ignite, you have 20 additional Endurance Threshold against that enemy's attacks.
You will always reach your target after 1.5 seconds, but modifiers to travel speed affect the maximum possible distance.
Runic Invocation penetrates elemental resistances per point of Intelligence.
Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze enemies and a higher chance to critically strike.
Frost Claw deals additional spell damage based on your added melee damage.
The type of added melee damage determines the type of spell damage gained. For example if you have +30 Melee Void Damage, Frost Claw will gain +10 Spell Void Damage.
Frost Claw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and its explosion deals damage in a larger area. These bonuses are doubled if you have at least 1200 ward.
Frost Claw costs less mana and you have a chance to regain mana when you cast it.
Chance to gain 12 mana on Cast

Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze enemies and a higher chance to critically strike.
Frost Claw deals additional spell damage based on your added melee damage.
The type of added melee damage determines the type of spell damage gained. For example if you have +30 Melee Void Damage, Frost Claw will gain +10 Spell Void Damage.
Frost Claw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and its explosion deals damage in a larger area. These bonuses are doubled if you have at least 1200 ward.
Frost Claw costs less mana and you have a chance to regain mana when you cast it.
Chance to gain 12 mana on Cast

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Chill chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to lightning, unless it has already been converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Frostbite chance from all sources is converted to shock chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
Frost Claw hits have a chance to {Slow} and {Chill}.
You have increased cast speed with Frost Claw and when you cast Frost Claw you cleanse a random set of negative ailments on you.
All stacks of a chosen negative ailment are cleansed at once. Negative ailments include, but are not limited to, ignite, chill, slow, poison, and shock.
Frost Claw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and its hits have a chance to inflict {Frostbite}.
Frostbite, chill, slow, ignite, and shock inflicted by Frost Claw last longer.

You have a chance to cast {Elemental Nova} when Frost Claw's projectiles reach the target.

This consumes mana equal to Elemental Nova's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.
The chance is once per cast, not once per projectile.
Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze based on its critical strike chance.
Freeze Rate Multiplier per 1% Crit Chance
If you have an active {Ice Barrage} when you hit an enemy with Frost Claw, that Ice Barrage fires faster for the rest of its duration, up to a maximum.
This fire rate modifier is multiplicative with other modifiers.
Ice Barrage Rate of Fire on Hit

You have a chance to cast {Ice Barrage} in the target direction when you cast Frost Claw.

This consumes mana equal to Ice Barrage's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.

When you are hit by an enemy you have a chance to retaliate with Frost Claw.

When you cast Frost Claw by any method you gain Ward.
When you cast Frost Claw you gain ward based on its Freeze Rate Multiplier.
Addtional Freeze Rate Multiplier from this tree, including from Cold and Calculating, is taken into account for this node.
When you use a Melee Attack and hit at least one enemy you have a chance to cast Frost Claw at it, or at another nearby enemy if the hit killed the original target.
Gain a stack of Frozen Sleeper every second up to a maximum. When you cast Frost Claw it consumes all stacks to gain cold penetration per stack and release an {Ice Spiral} for each stack.
Ice Spirals on Cast per Stack
When you cast {Ice Spirals} there is a chance that the number of projectiles will be doubled and they'll all deal double damage.
The chance is calculated when Ice Spirals are cast and either applies to all of them or none of them with each cast.
For each active {Ice Spiral}, Glacier gains increased area and additional cold penetration, and Snap Freeze gains increased area and freeze duration.
These bonuses apply based on the number of your Ice Spirals that exist when you begin casting Glacier or Snap Freeze.
Glacier Cold Penetration per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Area per Ice Spiral
Snap Freeze Freeze Duration per Ice Spiral

Directly casting Frost Claw causes it to recast from the target location to a location 4 meters behind it. The second can hit the same enemies as the first cast.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} causes a third cast. The third cast can hit the same enemies as the first and second casts.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.
The third cast is aimed at a location 4 meters behind the second cast.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} targets a random enemy that is 3 to 9 meters away from the original target location.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

When the second cast from {On Through The Snow} is released, Frost Claw is also cast back towards you.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.

Frost Claw has 2 additional projectiles allowing it to hit enemies in a wider area on the way to the target.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

Frost Claw no longer creates a burst of damage at the target, but its projectiles are now staggered and they can all hit the same target.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.
Frost Claw has a lower mana cost and it deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to frozen enemies, but its projectiles are slower.
Frost Claw's projectiles are faster and it costs less mana.

The effect of this node is not rounded. If it granted you 1 ward additional ward per 25 intelligence, but you only had 10 intelligence, it would grant 0.4 additional ward.
Poison Self on Cast -> Armor Shred
The armour modifiers from individual stacks are not multiplicative with each other, just with all armour modifiers from other sources.
Greater Fragment Chance per 10 Current Mana
For example if the threshold is 6 and only 2 cursed enemies remain in the area after casting Soul Feast up to 4 other enemies in the area will be Marked for Death.
Marks For Death to reach threshold
For each {Curse} on the target, Soul Feast hits grant more ward and deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Soul Feast inflicts {Withering} on hit, an ailment which increases the curse damage taken.
Chaos Bolts has increased cast speed, and its projectiles travel faster.
Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance to inflict enemies with damned.

Chaos Bolts' base necrotic damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

Damned chance from all sources are converted to bleed chance for Chaos Bolts.

Swaps Chaos Bolts' {Necrotic} tag for a {Physical} tag.
Chaos Bolts costs less mana, but now consumes a portion of your current health and ward on cast.
Chaos Bolts deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) equal to a portion of your missing health.

Hitting your minions with Chaos Bolts grants them any damned, ignite, bleed or frostbite chance that Chaos Bolts gains from this tree.

This buff lasts 10 seconds, and does not stack.
Ailment Chances to Minions on Hit

Directly casting Chaos Bolts extends the duration of your active {Cthonic Fissures} and {Infernal Shades}. 

This effect has a 3 second cooldown.
Chaos Bolts' hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to damned, ignited, bleeding and frostbitten.
Hitting enemies inflicted with multiple of these ailments, will cause the damage modifier to stack.
Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance to ignite enemies.

Chaos Bolts' base fire damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to fire damage. It now has a chance to freeze enemies.

Ignite chance from all sources are converted to frostbite chance for Chaos Bolts.

Swaps Chaos Bolts' {Fire} tag for a {Cold} tag.

Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance to chill enemies. 

Additionally, Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance to deal double hit damage to chilled enemies.
Chance for Double Hit Damage against Chilled

Chaos Bolts' hits have a higher chance to freeze enemies per 1% uncapped cold resistance.

Additionally, Chaos Bolts deals more physical damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 4% uncapped cold resistance.
Freeze Rate Multiplier per 1% Cold Res
Physical Damage over Time per 4% Cold Res
Chaos Bolts explosions have a chance to deal damage in a much large area.
Chaos Bolts' hits now have a chance to fear enemies, but the projectiles target a wider area.
Chaos Bolts releases additional projectiles, but it has a higher mana cost.
Chaos Bolts' projectiles have a chance to consume additional mana causing it to deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Whenever mana is consumed by the {Mana Anarchy} node, your minions gain additional spell damage based on your maximum mana and they also gain increased cast speed.

This buff lasts 13 seconds.

Chaos Bolts no longer shoots multiple projectiles, but now instead shoots one large projectile causing it to deal more damage (multiplicative with other multipliers) in a larger area. 

These effects scale with the amount of projectiles Chaos Bolts would have shot.
Chaos Bolts' hitting one of your {Volatile Zombies} causes it to instantly explode. Zombies exploding this way deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
{Volatile Zombies} exploding from the {Volatile Kindling} node deal even more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area and poisons enemies.

Whenever you cast Chaos Bolts you gain a stack of {Destructive Intensity} up to a maximum.

Each stack reduces the target area and improves Chaos Bolts base critical strike chance.

If you go 2 seconds without gaining a stack, all stacks are lost.
Chaos Bolts deals more damage and its hits deal more damage to {Cursed} enemies (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance per Dexterity to cause you to attack with {Harvest} from the location of enemies hit, hitting them and enemies around them.

This effect can occur a maximum of 3 times per second.
Chaos Bolts' hits against {Cursed} enemies regain health and mana.

Chaos Bolts' hits against {Cursed} enemies has a chance to cause you to recast Chaos Bolts from their location towards another nearby enemy, consuming a portion of its mana cost. 

The recast has a minimum range of 5 meters, and will not cast at enemies within that range.

Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance per Intelligence to cast {Rip Blood} at target enemies.

This effect can occur a maximum of 2 times per second.
Chaos Bolts hits refreshes the duration of your {Curses} on them and grants you health per curse.

Chaos Bolts' hits against {Cursed} enemies casts {Bone Curse} at their location.

This effect has a 6 second cooldown.
Ghostflame has a chance to inflict damned and ignite enemies each second.

Ghostflames's base necrotic and fire damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to necrotic or fire damage.

Damned and ignite chance from all sources are converted to bleed chance per second.

Ghostflame now drains a portion of your health each second.

Swaps Ghostflames's {Necrotic} and {Fire} tags for a {Physical} tag.

You leech a portion of the physical damage you deal as health while channeling Ghostflame.

A portion of your global bleed chance on hit now applies as bleed chance per second for Ghostflame.
Bleed Chance -> Bleed Chance per second

Damaging an enemy with Ghostflame causes you to cast {Marrow Shards} towards them.

This effect can occur a maximum number of times each second.
Max Marrow Shards per second against 1 target

Increases the frequency of {Marrow Shards} per second from the {Feast of Bones} node.

Improves the {Marrow Shards'} base critical strike chance cast this way.
Ghostflame has increased ignite and damned duration per point of Intelligence.

A portion of your global ignite chance on hit now applies as ignite chance per second for Ghostflame.

A portion of your global damned chance on hit now applies as damned chance per second for Ghostflame.
Ignite Chance -> Ignite Chance per second
Damned Chance -> Damned Chance per second

After channelling Ghostflame for a short duration, the skull releases a terrible screech, cursing all nearby enemies with {Decrepify}.

Ghost Flame gains the {Curse} tag, but its direct damage still does not scale with increased curse damage.
The screech from the {Decrepit Mortals} node has a shorter delay before being released and a larger area.
The screech from the {Decrepit Mortals} node now also fears enemies and Ghostflame deals more direct damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to feared enemies.
Ghostflame deals more damage, but has a higher channel cost (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
You take less damage from ignited, damned or bleeding enemies while channeling Ghostflame, but it costs more mana to channel.
Ghostflame deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.

You leech a portion of the damage you deal as health against {Cursed} enemies while channeling Ghostflame.

Additionally, Ghostflame has a chance to inflict Frailty each second.

When you stop channeling Ghostflame after having channeled for at least 1 second, you gain the {Haste} buff for a short duration.

Additionally, when you stop channeling you gain ward per {Cursed} enemy.
Ward Gain per Cursed enemy after Channeling

After channeling for 1.1 seconds Ghostflame detaches itself, and persists for short duration without you needing to channel.

This effect ends early if you start channeling Ghostflame again.
The duration from the {Memorial of Flames} node is longer, but Ghostflame now has an upfront mana cost.
The detached Ghostflame now slowly seeks out nearby enemies.
After channeling for 1 second, Ghostflame consumes a portion of your current ward, and it deals damage in a larger area per ward consumed.

For each 200 ward consumed by the {Silver Oblation} node you now cast a {Stygian Beam} at a nearby enemy dealing spell necrotic damage.

Enemies cannot be hit by more than 1 beam.
Current Health Drain per second per stack
Ignite Chance -> Minion Ignite Chance per stack
Current Mana -> Ward Gain while Channeling
Dodge Rating per Intelligence while Channeling
Flame energy builds up on enemies within the beam, causing an explosion that deals fire damage around them after 3 seconds of taking damage.
3 seconds if the target stays in the beam.

All your {Wraiths}, {Spectres}, and {Revenants} gain a stack of {Spirit Kindling} every 0.5 seconds while they are inside Ghostflame's area of effect.

Each stack of {Spirit Kindling} lasts 5 seconds, drains health per second, and grants increased movement speed, melee attack speed, and ignite chance equal to a portion of your ignite chance.
Ghostflame now deals damage in a full circle around you, but it has a higher channel cost.
You now move towards the target location while channeling Ghostflame with increased movement speed, but it has a higher channel cost.
Ghostflame has a lower channel cost, but you move slower while channeling Ghostflame.
You move faster while channeling Ghostflame.
Ghostflame deals more direct damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per second you spend channeling Ghostflame.
Ghostflame has a lower channel cost and you gain a portion of your current mana as ward each second while channeling Ghostflame.
You gain additional dodge rating per intelligence while channeling Ghostflame, as well as more dodge rating (multiplicative with other modifiers) while channeling Ghostflame.
All of your dodge rating is converted to armor while channeling Ghostflame.

Profane Veil's base necrotic damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

Profane Form now has a chance to ignite enemies each second.

Swaps Profane Form's {Necrotic} tag for a {Fire} tag.
Chill stacks on Cursed Rares and Bosses
Ward Gain per 15% Uncapped Necrotic Res
Ward Gain on Dodge per Dex
Portion of Minion health to Ward Gain
Bone Curse on you on Enter or Exit
Bone Curse at Target Location with Forked Tongue
Damned Spreads with Mark for Death
Poison Spreads with Mark for Death
Profane Orb Frequency per 1% Cast Speed
Penance Damage per 1% of Damage Reflected
Chance to replace Spirits with Chaos Bolts
Infernal Shade deals its Remaining Damage
Necrotic Damage per 1% Added Crit Multi
Profane Veil's area has a chance to slow enemies each second. Additionally, you leech a portion of the global damage you deal as health while in Profane Veil.
While in Profane Veil you gain a percentage of your damned, bleed, poison, slow and ignite chance on hit as chance to ignite per second for Profane Form.

You move faster equal to a portion of your chance to ignite on hit up to a maximum, but Profane Veil has a shorter duration per chance to ignite on hit up to a maximum.

Profane Veil gains the {Traversal} tag.

When you first damage an enemy with Profane Veil you have a chance to cast {Infernal Shade} on them.

This effect can occur a maximum amount of times per cast of Profane Veil.
Profane Veil Immobilizes {Cursed} enemies for a short duration, but Bosses and Rare enemies will instead be chilled, rather than Immobilized.
You deal more global damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to {Cursed} enemies while in Profane Veil.

Directly casting a {Curse} skill reduces Profane Veil's remaining cooldown.

This effect can occur a maximum number of times.
Profane Veil affects a larger area.

Profane Veil gains additional effects depending on the weapon type you are wielding. 

{Staff}: Increased area.
{Wand}: More damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Scepter}: Increased duration.
{Dagger}: Increased movement speed.
{Axe}: Kill threshold.

You now fear nearby enemies for a short duration when you exit Profane Veil. Additionally, Profane Veil deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to feared or slowed enemies for the same duration.
Damage reduction from armor now also applies to damage over time taken while in Profane Veil.

When a {Wandering Spirit} is revealed inside Profane Veil's area the {Spirit} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area for the rest of the {Spirit's} duration. 

Additionally, for each {Spirit} revealed inside Profane Veil's area you gain ward scaling with your necrotic resistance.
You gain ward per dexterity when you dodge while in Profane Veil.
Profane Veil lasts longer and you move faster while in it.

While in Profane Veil you move faster and it has a shorter cooldown, but you can no longer change direction while concealed and it has a shorter duration without being able to extend its duration.

Profane Veil gains the {Traversal} tag.
Profane Veil's area now consumes your minions, and consuming minions this way grants you ward equal to a portion of their maximum health as well as extending its remaining duration by a small amount.

You now summon {Volatile Zombies} when you exit Profane Veil equal to the number of minions consumed up to a maximum from the {Vampiric Pool} node.

{Zombies} summoned deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area per Intelligence.

The benefits from the {Vampiric Pool} and {Apocalypse} nodes now also trigger when you kill enemies with Profane Veil.

The limit from {Vampiric Pool} remains the same.

Profane Veil's base necrotic damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

Profane Veil deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 10% global Bleed chance on hit.

Swaps Profane Veil's {Necrotic} tag for a {Physical} tag.
You now cast {Bone Curse} when you enter and exit Profane Veil.
If you have the {Forked Tongue} node then this also casts {Bone Curse} at the second area.
You now create a secondary Profane Veil area that is detached from you and moves towards enemies, but Profane Veil now has a higher mana cost.
The secondary area has the same movespeed as you, enemies can be damaged by both areas at once and it ends the end at the same time.
The secondary area from {Forked Tongue} is now created at the target location, but Profane Veil costs additional mana.
Profane Veil costs less mana and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Profane Veil's area now applies {Mark for Death} to enemies, but entering Profane Veil applies {Mark for Death} to you.
When you apply {Mark for Death} to an enemy or yourself by any method, all your stacks of damned and poison on them spread to a number of nearby enemies.
While in Profane Veil, you consume 6 mana to cast a {Profane Orb} each second that seek out enemies.
The Orb is affected by damage conversion nodes.

Increases the frequency of {Profane Orb} per 1% increased cast speed for {Curses} you have. Additionally, {Profane Orb} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per {Curse} on the target.

Profane Veil's area inflicts {Penance} to enemies, which is a {Curse} that causes enemies to take fire damage when they hit a target. Additionally Penance deals more damage per 1% of Damage Reflected (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Chthonic Fissure costs less mana and it refunds some of its mana cost if there is a cursed player, rare enemy or boss within 20 meters when you cast it.

This effect also works if you are Cursed.
{Tormenting} an ignited enemy spreads all of your ignite stacks from it to a number of other enemies around it.

Chthonic Fissure now creates a secondary Fissure in the opposite direction that casts its own {Spirits}. Enemies can take damage from both Fissures if they stand on both, but Chthonic Fissure costs significantly more mana.

{Spirits} now have a chance to be replaced by a cast of {Chaos Bolts} that consumes a portion of its mana cost and scales with your {Chaos Bolts} tree.

{Chaos Bolts} cast this way have a chance to have no additional projectiles.
When {Tormented} enemies die they have a chance to release the {Spirit} to inflict and {Torment} another enemy within 8 meters.
The {Spirits'} hits shred enemy necrotic resistance.
The {Spirits'} hits shred enemy fire resistance.

When {Spirits} {Torment} an enemy afflicted by an {Infernal Shade} they consume the Shade, causing it to instantly deal its remaining damage and increases all existing Chthonic Fissures' frequency of releasing {Spirits} up to a maximum.
Chthonic Fissure and ailments it inflicts deal more necrotic damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 1% Added Critical Strike Multiplier.
{Torment} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) based on your uncapped necrotic resistance.

Chthonic Fissure deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to rare enemies and bosses.

This effect is doubled if the enemy or you are cursed.

{Spirits} now target you instead of enemies, and whenever you are {Tormented} or {Torment's} duration on you is refreshed, you gain additional spell damage and Endurance Threshold.

This buff can stack and has the same duration as {Torment}, but persists if {Torment} is cleansed.
The Fissure deals more direct damage for each second it is active (multiplicative with other modifiers) and you leech a portion of the damage it does.
Fissure Direct Damage bonus per Second

You can now have additional Fissures active at a time, but {Spirits} are released less frequently.

If a Fissure is placed over an existing one, the new Fissure closes after 1 second.
Chthonic Fissure deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but it no longer releases {Spirits}.

Fissures now cast {Volatile Zombies} every 2 seconds, but consumes mana equal to a portion of {Volatile Zombies} mana cost.

{Volatile Zombie} cast this way has a chance to not create multiple {Zombies} if it would otherwise.

Chthonic Fissure deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This effect is doubled if you are cursed.
The Fissure's initial impact now hits enemies dealing spell fire damage, with added damage applying at 300% effectiveness for this hit.
This hit can be converted to to physical by {Blood Gulch}, or to necrotic by {Valley of Defilement}.

The Fissure's initial hit now deals additional spell damage with a higher chance to stun per 2% ignite chance.

This effects scales with bleed or poison if the Fissure is converted to physical or poison respectively.
Ailment chance is counted before being converted.

Fissure Increased Stun Chance per 2% Ignite Chance
The Fissure's initial hit now Curse enemies with {Acid Skin}, which causes them to take poison damage over time and a chance to be critically struck by any hit.

Your hits against enemies cursed by {Acid Skin} have additional base critical strike chance per 10% of your global increased poison damage.

This bonus critical strike chance is not affected by other critical strike chance modifiers.
Bonus Crit Chance per 10% Inc Poison Damage
The Fissure's initial impact now spreads all of your ignite stacks from the target to nearby enemies up to a maximum.

When directly casting Chthonic Fissure you now travel along the Fissure as it opens, but this adds a cooldown to Chthonic Fissure.

Chthonic Fissure gains the {Traversal} tag.

The Fissure gains a percentage of your global chance to ignite on hit as ignite chance per second. 

Additionally, the Fissure has a base chance to ignite enemies each second.

Chthonic Fissure's base fire damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Ignite chance from all sources are converted to bleed chance per second for the Fissure.

Swaps Chthonic Fissures' {Fire} tag for a {Physical} tag.
{Torment} lasts longer and any ignite, poison and bleed inflicted by Chthonic Fissure also last longer.
{Torment} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to ignited, poisoned or bleeding enemies.

Chthonic Fissure's base fire damage over time is converted to poison. Consequently this damage scales with increases to poison damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Ignite chance from all sources is converted to poison chance per second for the Fissure.

Swaps Chthonic Fissures' {Fire} tag for a {Poison} tag.

You deal increased fire damage and have a chance to ignite on hit with fire skills, and you deal increased necrotic damage and have a chance to inflict damned on hit with necrotic skills.

These effects also apply to your minions.

You gain additional ward decay threshold and you gain ward on kill. 

You also gain ward per 2 points of Intelligence while channeling a skill.
You take less damage while channeling and deal more damage to damned enemies while channeling (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
You gain ward and mana when you use a potion.
Chaining an enemy to the ground deals necrotic damage over time and immobilizes them.
When you directly cast a {Curse} skill you also afflict a number of enemies around the target location with {Anguish}.

Your hits have a chance to deal double damage.

Additionally your hits have a chance to deal double damage per {Curse} on the target.
The Chains from the {Chains of Ruin} node last longer. Additionally damned that you inflict last longer.

You have a chance to gain {Haste} for 2 seconds when you kill a {Cursed} enemy or hit a {Cursed} boss or rare enemy.

Additionally {Haste} on you has increased effect.

{Witchfire} that you inflict deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 1% global chance to ignite with fire skills and per 1% global chance to inflict damned with necrotic skills.

Additionally while you have {Ignite} or {Damned Overload} your hits spread {Witchfire} to a number of enemies within 10 meters.
You have increased health and you deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of each negative ailment on you up to a maximum of 90.
Direct damage modifiers do not affect damage of ailments and subskills from Ghostflame.
Direct damage modifiers do not affect damage of ailments and subskills from Ghostflame.
Direct damage modifiers do not affect damage of ailments and subskills from Chthonic Fissure.
Using a Traversal skill puts all other traversal skills on cooldown and traversal skills' cooldowns do not recover while you are using a traversal skill.
Scrap Metal stacks have a base duration of 10 seconds, but modifiers to Forged Weapon duration now apply to Scrap Metal duration as well.
Your Falcon's other hits now have a chance to inflict {Falconer's Mark}.

When you consume a {Falconer's Mark} your Falcon gains additional melee damage equal to a portion of your added melee damage, throwing damage, bow damage, or spell damage depending on the type of ability used to consume the mark.

This effect lasts 10 seconds and can stack a limited number of times.
Stronger stacks can only be replaced by weaker stacks if their remaining duration is less than 2 seconds.

Your Falcon now throws {Acid Flasks} for you. {Acid Flasks} thrown by the Falcon scale with your stats and your {Acid Flask} tree, but can apply {Falcon's Mark} from {Marking Strikes}.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of your {Acid Flask's} mana cost and has a 6 second cooldown.
Your Falcon has additional {Acid Flask} charges.
When you directly use {Acid Flask} your Falcon has a chance to gain additional {Acid Flask} charges.
Your Falcon deals more global damage per character level and per point of Dexterity (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

Your Falcon deals more global damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). 

This effect is tripled for {Falcon Strikes}.
Your Falcon now more heavily prioritizes enemies close to you during combat.
Your Falcon now heavily prioritizes enemies close to the target location of your most recent melee, throwing or bow attack.

Your Falcon deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This effect is tripled against moving targets.
When your Falcon hits an enemy it has a chance to drop a {Black Arrow} at its location.
A portion of your chance to inflict bleed, poison, shred armor, shred resistances, and slow on hit now also apply to your Falcon's hits.

Your Falcon now uses {Feather Knives} at nearby enemies. This effect has a 10 second cooldown.

Your Falcon also throws a single Feather Knife whenever you consume {Falconer's Mark}.
A portion of your global throwing attack speed now applies to the Falcon's {Feather Knives} ability as increased cooldown recovery speed.
A portion of the highest of your global increased melee damage, increased throwing damage, or increased bow damage now apply to all your Falcon's damage.
Your critical strike chance modifiers now also apply to your {Falcon}, but at a reduced ratio.

When you use a melee, throwing, or bow attack and critically strike at least one enemy, your Falcon gains a portion of your global melee, throwing, or bow critical strike multiplier respectively for 10 seconds.

This effect can occur at most twice every two second.
When you directly use a melee, throwing, or bow attack and hit at least one enemy, you reduce a portion of {Falcon Strike's} remaining cooldown.
After the first hit of {Falcon Strike} your Falcon lets out a {Screech} which slows and applies {Frailty} to enemies around it.

Your Falcon's {Screech} now also fears enemies it hits.

Additionally, your Falcon moves to you when a hit leaves you at low health, and this effect will interrupt any skill it is currently using.

This effect can occur at most every 10 seconds.
{Falcon Strikes} hits addtional times and your {Falcon} has increased melee attack speed.

{Falcon Strikes} instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).

If you have a similar effect from an item or passive node then the highest threshold is used.
{Falcon Strikes} now cause your Falcon to swoop down in a line in front of you attacking all enemies in that line rather than hitting enemies individually.

A portion of your global increased area for area skills now applies to the width of {Falcon Strikes}.

Additionally, {Falcon Strikes} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if you have directly used an area skill in the past 8 seconds.

If {Falcon Strikes} kills an enemy it will hit an additional time.

Additionally, when {Falcon Strikes} hits a boss or rare enemy it has a chance to hit an additional time.

When your Falcon kills an enemy or hits a boss or rare enemy it restores health and mana to you based on your total attributes. This effect can occur from hits a limited number of times per 3 seconds.

When you consume {Falconer's Mark} you gain the {Falcon's Wake} buff for a short duration. 

This buff grants you increased movement speed, attack and cast speed.
Consuming a {Falconer's Mark} instantly recovers {Falcon Strike's} cooldown, but it now also costs additional mana.
